title: 1265 PRE-LOAD
description: **Before you apply to a job, select your language preference from the options available at the top right of this page.** Explore your next opportunity at a Fortune Global 500 organization. Envision innovative possibilities, experience our rewarding culture, and work with talented teams that help you become better every day. We know what it takes to lead UPS into tomorrow—people with a unique combination of skill \+ passion. If you have the qualities and drive to lead yourself or teams, there are roles ready to cultivate your skills and take you to the next level. **Job Description** **Job Summary** This position is responsible for loading, unloading, shipping, receiving, scanning, sorting, and stocking packages. He/She may utilize heavy machinery to complete tasks. This position performs other tasks as assigned in a fast\-paced environment. **Responsibilities** * Learns and properly executes UPS package handling methods. * Loads and unloads UPS packages into trailers or package cars . **Qualifications** * Ability to lift up to 70 lbs./32 kgs. * Ability to read and memorize postal codes * Availability to work flexible shirt hours, up to 5 days per week * Warehouse experience \- Preferred **Employee Type** Permanent UPS is committed to providing a workplace free of discrimination, harassment, and retaliation.

title: A Fresh Start for Your Career in Recruitment Consulting
description: * **Channel your drive into a role that fuels a company in rapid expansion.** * **Your effort sets the pace – fast and completely performance\-based career progression.** * **Join a business that rewards ambition and invests in your future.** What if your career moved forward exactly as fast as you’re willing to push it? What if you didn’t have to wait for someone else’s approval to take the next step? At **Access** , your results speak louder than anything else. Here, **progression isn’t about time served, it’s about performance** . If you bring energy, resilience, and hunger to succeed, there’s no ceiling on what you can achieve. Maybe you’re already in recruitment and ready for a step up. Or maybe you’re looking for a new challenge: a role where ambition, people skills, and results are truly rewarded. Either way, this is your chance to build something exciting. **What the role involves:** As a Recruitment Consultant, you’ll be backed by a leadership team dedicated to helping you grow quickly and confidently. Every day brings variety and purpose: * Building relationships with clients and candidates across the healthcare sector * Managing live vacancies from first briefing to final offer * Interviewing, qualifying, and advising candidates through each stage of the process **What’s in it for you:** * **Career Progression** \- Rise fast through a clear, merit\-based pathway into senior and leadership positions. * **Continuous Development** \- Access expert mentorship, hands\-on training, and specialist knowledge to accelerate your career. * **Recognition \& Rewards** \- Every milestone matters. Expect real recognition, added responsibility, and outstanding financial rewards. The **average total compensation for year 1 is more than €45k, and for year 2 it’s over €75k** . Of course, due to our uncapped commission structure, top performers earn much more than this from the jump. * **Leadership Academy** \- Exclusive access to our Leadership Academy for advanced training, certifications, and networking with top performers. **You’ll be a great fit if you have:** * At least 1 year of experience in a fast\-moving environment (any sector) * A proactive, ambitious mindset and the confidence to take ownership of your growth * Strong communication skills and a natural drive to succeed **Your success story could look like Rian’s:** In just **24 months** , he progressed from Recruitment Consultant **to Associate Manager** , building a high\-performing team and shaping his own leadership path. In two years, you could be his peer, and you could be telling this same story to your friends, but this time about yourself. For more information, contact César Toscano at **\+353 1 572 1161** or email your CV to **cesar.toscano@accesshealthcare.ie**

title: AI Builder, Emerging Talent - Arabic Market
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* **About Futureforce University Recruiting** Our Futureforce University Recruiting program is dedicated to attracting, retaining and cultivating talent. Our interns and new graduates work on real projects that affect how our business runs, giving them the opportunity to make a tangible impact on the future of our company. With offices all over the world, our recruits have the chance to collaborate and connect with fellow employees on a global scale. We offer job shadowing, mentorship programs, talent development courses, and much more. **Job Category** Software Engineering **Job Details** **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **AI Builder, Emerging Talent \- Arabic Market** Technical foundation required. Builder mindset non\-negotiable. Salesforce is hiring AI Builders, people who are early in their career but not early in their ambition. As part of our first\-of\-its\-kind **AI Builder New Grad cohort** , you'll be embedded directly with customer\-facing teams, designing, developing, and deploying **Agentic solutions** for the world’s Fortune 500\. We are at the beginning of the agentic era, and you will be at the keyboard building the solutions that define how the world's largest companies operate. You’ll deliver real\-time customer impact from day one. Working in a builder pod, a cross\-functional team that collaborates to design, build, and deploy AI solutions for real customers. It's a built\-in support system to ensure you're set up to win. Honestly, you're already thinking about what you'd build here… **The AI Builder Experience** * AI\-Powered Development: You'll be developing real customer solutions with the best\-in\-class suite of leading\-edge AI tools: Including Cursor, Claude, and our coding products like Vibes, to accelerate development. * Dedicated Mentorship: You won’t be building in a vacuum. You’ll be part of a program focused on hands\-on technical skill development, shadowing opportunities, and meaningful networking. You’ll be paired with a dedicated onboarding buddy and have the opportunity to partner with senior developers. * Skill Acceleration: This role is designed to develop both your technical depth and your sales skills, ensuring you can lead high\-level conversations with stakeholders. **What You'll Actually Be Building** * Develop AI agents that take real actions for real companies. This is not a side project. Agentforce is live, in production, running for some of the biggest companies on the planet. * Design Agent Intelligence: Design how agents think: prompts, reasoning, tool calls, and the entire architecture, leveraging AI/LLM technologies and the Agentforce platform. * Support the entire Technical Lifecycle: Collaborate with other Builders to architect, build, debug, and deploy high\-quality, scalable production systems using JavaScript, Python, and Apex. * Drive Strategic Impact: Partner with clients to turn business challenges into agentic solutions. You’ll use your consultative skills to ensure our tech meets real\-world needs * Iterate and Deploy: Test it, break it, fix it, ship it. Repeat. You will own your work from day one. **You’re Our Builder If…** * You have a strong background in Computer Science or a related engineering discipline. * You’re an AI native who has been deep in LLMs and prompt engineering. * You are fluent in React, TypeScript, GraphQL, Python, OOP and Real\-Time Infra * You can hold your own in a room with our sales teams and customers. * You write high\-quality code and evaluate AI outputs with engineering rigor. * You thrive in the group chat that’s building something, not just talking about it. * You're ready to grow faster here than anywhere else \- nobody has 10 years of experience in this new frontier, which means your ideas carry real weight from day one. * You bring sharp problem\-solving instincts, relentless initiative, and a get\-things\-done mentality. * You are excited to travel 25\-50% of the time, working directly alongside our customers to see your code come to life in their environments. * Fluency required in Arabic and English Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. ********Posting Statement******** Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

title: AI Centre of Excellence Platform Owner
description: **What is the opportunity?** The AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) is the Bank's engine for safe, responsible and scalable AI adoption. It defines the strategy, standards and governance that guide how AI is built and used, while providing the shared platforms, tools and expertise that power delivery across the organisation. Your mission is to shape the vision, build out and day to day excellence of the BOI AI Platform turning it into a powerful, shared, self service capability that accelerates AI across the entire enterprise. You'll define robust platform services and reusable AI patterns that form the backbone of every AI initiative. You lead platform engineering with clarity and authority, ensuring the platform is resilient, compliant, cost efficient and primed for innovation. As the technical north star, you translate AI CoE strategy into real world delivery, working closely with the Cloud CoE to provide CSSP aligned infrastructure that scales with ambition. In essence, you're the driving force that transforms AI strategy into a reliable, repeatable and future ready enterprise capability! **In This Role, You Will** * Lead the strategic roadmap, design, and evolution of the BOI AI Platform as a shared enterprise service. * Define and govern reusable AI archetypes, platform patterns, and shared services to enable safe, consistent, and accelerated AI delivery. * Own the platform engineering team, setting technical direction and ensuring high quality engineering execution in partnership with the Cloud CoE. * Ensure platform compliance with security, regulatory, and architecture standards, while driving adoption across pathfinder pods and business units. * Lead AI and cloud FinOps, including cost optimisation, capacity planning, and budget governance, alongside platform SLAs, availability, monitoring, and incident response. * Oversee evaluation and onboarding of AI tooling, models, and third party components, maintaining strong documentation, knowledge transfer, and responsible AI practices. **What will make you stand out?** * Deep expertise in cloud based AI/ML platform engineering, with experience delivering enterprise scale platforms in regulated environments. * Strong grounding in cloud architecture, governance, information security, data governance and financial?'services regulatory requirements. * Validated product ownership and partner management, with a track record of shaping platform roadmaps and driving adoption. * Broad knowledge of AI/ML model lifecycle management, data engineering patterns and integration of AI systems into enterprise ecosystems. * Skilled engineering leader with strong mentorship abilities and experience building high performing technical teams. * Commercial competence in running cloud costs and platform budgets, paired with excellent communication skills for translating sophisticated concepts to diverse audiences. Experience working in regulated financial services or other complex enterprise environments is highly desirable. **Essential Qualifications** There are no specific qualifications or minimum educational requirements needed for this role. **More About The Team** Working hand in hand with business teams, the CoE identifies, prioritises and accelerates high value AI use cases that deliver measurable impact. It strikes the balance between innovation and regulatory field, ensuring every AI solution meets the Bank's highest expectations for ethics, risk management and control and ultimately turns AI into a trusted, enterprise wide capability. This is a hybrid role, based primarily in Dublin. We typically ask colleagues to spend a minimum of 8 days per month working in\-person to support teamwork and connection. Specific arrangements will be confirmed by your recruiter to ensure they meet the needs of the role and team. \#ij\-hybrid **Why work with us?** The Bank of Ireland company culture prioritises work\-life balance with an opportunity for flexible working, along with 25 days annual leave and excellent pension contributions. Family can mean different things to different people; we offer 6 months paid maternity leave, an innovative fertility and surrogacy policy and working parent supports. Your wellbeing is important to us; we have an employee assistance program, WebDoctor and financial wellbeing coaches available. We also encourage and support staff to pursue educational and professional qualifications to grow and enhance your career! **Key Competencies** * Manage Risk \- Leader * Take Ownership \- Leader * Better together \- Leader * Customer Focused \- Leader * Be Decisive \- Leader We're on a continuous journey to build an inclusive and diverse workplace. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, lived experience, abilities and perspectives. We provide reasonable accommodations at every stage of our recruitment process for disabilities, neurodivergence or medical conditions. If you require an accommodation please complete this form and one of our recruitment team members will be in touch via email. Any information provided will be treated as confidential within the recruitment team and used only for the purpose of determining and providing appropriate accommodations for the application and recruitment process. Where Agency assistance is required Bank of Ireland Recruitment Team will engage directly with suppliers. Unsolicited CVs / profiles supplied to Bank of Ireland by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this role.

title: AI Risk Analyst - 1LOD
description: What is the opportunity? The AI Centre of Excellence (CoE) is the Bank's powerhouse for **safe, responsible and scalable AI adoption** . It sets the vision, standards and governance that shape how AI is built and deployed across the organisation, while providing the platforms, expertise and partnership needed to turn ideas into high value outcomes. This role establishes a critical new capability focused on AI trustworthiness and performance. It defines how AI model outputs are tested, monitored and governed across the Bank, from accuracy and bias to hallucination and explainability. The opportunity is to shape responsible AI standards in practice, influence regulatory alignment, and build a scalable evaluation framework that underpins confidence in AI driven decision making across the organisation. **In This Role, You Will** * Own and operationalize the AI model evaluation framework across all archetypes, covering accuracy, bias, hallucination, explainability, robustness, ethical compliance and guardrail thresholds. * Lead end to end evaluation processes from pathfinder through production, developing quantitative and qualitative testing methodologies for generative AI, classical ML and decisioning models. * Handle LLMOps monitoring, including performance dashboards, drift detection, degradation alerts and retraining triggers, ensuring outputs feed into 1LOD and 2LOD risk assessments, regulatory submissions and model risk documentation. * Produce governance ready evaluation reporting, contribute to the AI risk taxonomy and maintain alignment with evolving regulatory expectations and industry standards. * Build organisational capability through guidance, tooling and knowledge sharing, while applying evaluation frameworks to third party AI systems and vendor models. What will make you stand out? * Deep expertise in AI/ML model evaluation and monitoring including generative AI, LLMs and MLOps/LLMOps practices such as drift detection and lifecycle management. * Strong grounding in responsible AI principles (bias, explainability, fairness, ethics) and experience developing quantitative and qualitative evaluation frameworks. * Solid understanding of financial services AI regulation (EBA model risk guidelines, EU AI Act) with the ability to translate complex model behaviour into clear, actionable insights for non\-technical partners. * Excellent analytical, communication and collaboration skills, with experience partnering across risk, delivery and technology teams; financial services experience highly desirable. * Commitment to responsible innovation, ethical standards and continuous professional development. **Essential Qualifications** There are no specific qualifications or minimum educational requirements needed for this role. More about the team Working at the intersection of innovation and control, the CoE ensures the Bank can **unlock meaningful business impact from AI** while upholding the highest expectations of ethics, risk management and regulatory compliance. It's where pioneering capability meets focused execution enabling the Bank to harness AI with confidence, clarity and ambition. This is a hybrid role, based primarily in Dublin. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, lived experience, abilities and perspectives. We provide reasonable accommodations at every stage of our recruitment process for disabilities, neurodivergence or medical conditions. If you require an accommodation please complete this form and one of our recruitment team members will be in touch via email. title: AI Support Engineer - Dublin
description: **About The Team** OpenAI’s User Operations team shepherds our customers’ adoption of AI and ensures that our customers' product experience is nothing short of exceptional. We are building the very first post\-AGI support team. We resolve complex issues, provide technical guidance, and support customers in maximizing value and adoption from deploying our products. We work closely with Sales, Technical Success, Product, Engineering and others, to deliver the best possible experience to our customers at scale. OpenAI's customers represent a range of diverse backgrounds and maturity, from early\-stage startups to established global enterprises. **About The Role** We are looking for dedicated, experienced, and passionate individuals to help solve some of the most difficult problems faced by our customers and build our post\-AGI support team with us. In this role, you will interact directly with customers through support tickets and Slack messages, troubleshooting complex issues and resolving novel, often undefined technical problems while setting a positive precedent for the rest of the team. You’ll partner closely with cross\-functional teams to drive initiatives that reduce bugs, improve features, and build the systems that elevate our customer experience. Your work will bring us toward industry\-leading response times and service levels, while strengthening our internal customer feedback operations in an increasingly intricate space. You will help scale our support organization by improving operational processes and leveraging our own technology to build the next version of the support team in the new AI world. You will be crucial to the success of the most innovative, disruptive, and high\-scale AI solutions being built with OpenAI. If you thrive in environments that value impact, collaboration, and fast\-paced problem\-solving, you might be the perfect fit for our team We use a hybrid work model of 3 days in the office per week and offer relocation assistance to new employees. **You’ll Be Responsible For** * Work directly with customers, solving their most complex problems and providing ownership and education on the use of our platforms. * Be among the foremost experts on everything related to OpenAI products, even where our AI does not have the answer. * Be among the last line of defense before our core Product and Engineering teams, and partner with engineering and customer teams to resolve issues. * Use scripting and emerging AI capabilities to improve internal tooling and automate recurring processes. * Take learnings from resolving customer issues and create our approach to scaling these solutions, partnering with product and Go\-To\-Market teams. * Orchestrate agentic improvements to our operations that will level\-up our entire team. * Foster a supportive and productive work culture within the User Operations team. * Provide support coverage in on call shifts and during holidays and weekends based on business needs. * Note: each person in this role will be expected to work a standard 5x8 work week, with 1 of the days covering weekend shifts each week (e.g., work weeks covering Tuesday \- Saturday or Sunday \- Thursday) **You Might Thrive In This Role If You Have** * Have 8\+ years of experience in user operations, technical support, or support engineering roles, ideally within tech startups or fast\-paced environments. * Are comfortable using emerging technologies (ie. Codex, ChatGPT, OpenAI API, etc.) to script or engineer code (e.g., Python or similar) for automating repetitive tasks and integrating tools. * Have expert\-level SaaS troubleshooting skills with the ability to rapidly understand new technologies and complex concepts. * Naturally question established norms, skillfully identify root causes, and proactively drive innovation and process improvements. * Are among the very best critical thinkers, problem solvers, and communicators of complex technical issues in the industry. * Thrive in ambiguity, adapt rapidly to change, continuously learn, and proactively seek opportunities for growth. * Have proven experience building strong relationships with customers and cross\-functionally to drive resolution to complex issues. * Have a humble attitude, an eagerness to help others, and a desire to pick up whatever knowledge you're missing to make both your team and our customers succeed. * Have high horsepower, are adept at frequent context switching and working on multiple projects at once with expansive ownership and prioritization. * Preferred: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, another relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience. **Compensation, Benefits And Perks** This is a position with OpenAI Ireland Ltd., which controls the hiring and management of this position. Total compensation includes an annual salary, generous equity, and benefits. * Medical, dental, and vision insurance for you and your family * Mental health and wellness support * PRSA plan with 8% employer matching * Unlimited time off * Annual learning \& development stipend ($1,500 USD equivalent per year) **About OpenAI** OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general\-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement. Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US\-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non\-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations. To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non\-compliant, please submit a report through this form. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link. OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.

title: AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin (2 days pw onsite)
description: **AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin (2 days pw onsite)** My client is a SaaS multinational and has engaged IT Search to speak to a mid level AWS Engineer. * 6\+ years of experience * Knowledge of AWS Services * Exposure to Terraform and Kubernetes * Strong Python coding ability * CI/CD and Pipeline creation Happy to share more detail but the existing team has a high standard of engineer and the role comes with a very strong benefits package and work environment. Please note, we can only accept applications from candidates eligible to work in the EU as our client is unable to offer sponsorship.

title: AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)
description: **AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)** My client, based in Dublin City, is a leader in providing technology to Life Science regulatory processes and is continuing to expand its Platform team. They have partnered with IT Search to engage a mid\-level AWS DevOps Engineer to work alongside Engineering Heads and work on complex issues relating to automation, CI/CD, business logic, security and design matters. ***The role:*** * Develop strong internal relationships with Developers and Quality teams to deliver architecture on an ongoing basis, working closely with Architects to execute pipelines * Focus on areas such as security at scale, taking ownership of high quality output across deployment and create/manage/amend CI/CD pipelines to a deep level * Automation through (Python or similar) * There will be some involvement in incident response when required * Evidence based attitude to work, ability to back up ideas and recognise errors and change course accordingly * Proven experience of AWS Services, Pipelining, Kubernetes, Terraform and Python ***Role Requirements:*** * Approximately 5 years of commercial experience in a Platform/DevOps Environment * Ideally have worked in depth with Kubernetes Clusters (maintaining as well as building rather than simply deploying), AWS Services (as a preference), IaC (Terraform ideally), Python/Go on a daily basis for ongoing scripting and automation This is a high potential position with a well\-established firm to join a highly experienced and talented team, with a strong benefits package and work life balance. To learn more, please share your latest CV to arrange a confidential conversation, however, please note we can only accept applications from candidates eligible to work on a permanent basis in Ireland.

Based in our Dublin hub, you will lead the "agentic era" of technology, helping businesses transform through AI, Data, and CRM. Note: By applying to the Small Medium / Mid\-market Business Account Executive posting, recruiters and hiring managers who support multiple cloud offerings and verticals across the organization hiring Small Medium Business Account Executives will review your resume. Our goal is for you to apply once and have your resume reviewed by multiple hiring teams. **Key Requirements:** --------------------- * Language: Full professional fluency in English is mandatory. * Location: This is a Hybrid role based in Dublin, Ireland (Relocation assistance included). **Overview of the Role \- Your Mission** ---------------------------------------- As an Account Executive for the UK/I ESMB team, you own the end\-to\-end sales cycle. You will balance "whitespace" prospecting (new business) with strategic expansion within existing accounts. * Full Cycle Sales: Lead a dedicated customer set in the UK/I market, managing complex sales projects from lead generation to closing. * Strategic Growth: Drive performance against key metrics with a primary focus on new business acquisition and expansion. * Solution Selling: Utilize a consultative approach to identify business challenges and align Salesforce AI and Data solutions to customer goals. * Relationship Building: Develop trusted relationships with C\-level decision\-makers through regular digital engagement and face\-to\-face meetings. * Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Partner with Business Development, Pre\-sales, and Marketing to optimize the UK/I market funnel. **What We’re Looking For** **Required Qualifications :** * Demonstrated success of quota carrying, technology solution\-based direct sales experience * Territory \& Account Planning Strategies: Create territory and account plans to achieve sales objectives by effectively identifying and qualifying prospects and opportunities. * Research and Discovery: Uncovers a prospect’s current processes, business challenges, and strategic goals based on customer use cases and value hypotheses. * Solutioning: Identifies compelling value propositions that address customer needs by speech, presentation) and tools (Whiteboarding, Slack, Google Slides, Zoom). * Resource Application: Continuously runs toward results using the full capabilities of available resources and tools. * environment. * Track record of consistently achieving or surpassing quota. * Experience will be evaluated based on alignment to the core competencies for the role. **What’s In It For You (Benefits \& Growth)** * Personal Wellbeing: Private health insurance, life insurance, a retirement savings plan, and a monthly wellness allowance. * Flexibility: Flexible time off and leave policies, plus comprehensive parental support programs. * Career Development: Access to world\-class AI and sales training, mentorship, and a clear path for promotion within the global Salesforce ecosystem. * Giving Back: 7 paid volunteer days per year and donation matching for the causes you care about. * Perks: Employee discounts, inclusive resource groups, and a culture that celebrates Equality. Equality \& Inclusion Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer committed to building an inclusive workplace. We welcome applicants from all backgrounds and ensure reasonable accommodations are provided throughout the recruitment process where required. title: Allocation Analyst
description: Ranked as one of Ireland’s best workplaces, by ‘Great Place to Work’, Harvey Norman, is a high\-profile, leading\-edge retail brand with over 300 stores worldwide selling furniture, bedding, electrical, and computer goods. With 16 stores in Ireland, we are growing and have plenty of opportunities for talented people. If you think you have what it takes to thrive in a dynamic retail environment, we want to hear from you. **About this Role.** As a member of the Harvey Norman’s Integrated Inventory Planning Team and based onsite in Swords Corporate Office, you will manage stock allocations and replenishments to stores while supporting the planning function. Collaborating closely with the Merchandise Planner, you will analyse sales trends, monitor deliveries, and maintain optimal stock levels. **Responsibilities of the Allocations Analyst will include:** * Manage stock replenishment cycles and raise purchase orders in line with demand forecasts. * Collaborate with suppliers and buying teams to ensure stock availability, timely deliveries, and alignment with product life cycles. * Maintain accurate order books by monitoring delivery schedules, cancelling ages orders and proactively resolving supply issues. * Ensure optimal stock levels by maintaining availability of core lines while avoiding overstocking. * Monitor sales performance to identify opportunities for additional buys and mitigate risks from slow\-moving or excess stock. * Generate and analyse reports on categories, brands, and products to track inventory and margin performance across the division. * Develop expertise in internal reporting systems, becoming a super\-user of Excel\-based and SAP tools to enhance reporting capabilities. * Support data accuracy and administration, ensuring correct product coding and attributes while providing full administrative support to the Inventory team. **Requirements for the Allocations Analyst include:** * Strong proficiency in Excel, with the ability to manipulate data from multiple sources, build clear and insightful reports, and support data\-driven decision\-making. * A collaborative approach, with a focus on building trust and rapport across teams and suppliers. * Strategic and self\-driven, with the ability to take initiative and effectively prioritise workload in a dynamic environment. * Excellent organisational and time management skills, enabling effective performance in a fast\-paced and environment. **WHY PEOPLE JOIN US!** * We are dynamic and growing. * Fun, high energy work environment. * Culture of developing and promoting from within the company. * Our entrepreneurial spirit. * Generous staff discounts.

title: Application Analyst
description: **18 month fixed term contract** (until September 2027\) An opportunity has arisen for an experienced ICT Applications Analyst to support the delivery, enhancement, and ongoing management of enterprise application solutions within a complex operational environment. The successful candidate will act as the lead Applications Analyst across key business and technology initiatives, working closely with stakeholders to identify opportunities for process improvement and technology enablement. Key Responsibilities * Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand operational requirements and identify opportunities to improve efficiency through technology and process enhancement. * Gather, analyse, and document user and system requirements for application solutions. * Evaluate business needs and design appropriate application solutions aligned with organisational architecture and technology standards. * Coordinate procurement activities in accordance with relevant governance and compliance requirements, including engagement with third\-party vendors and service providers. * Ensure effective integration of new and existing systems across the wider technology landscape. * Lead and coordinate user acceptance testing (UAT) activities to ensure solution quality and business readiness. * Manage the implementation of application solutions, including project planning, stakeholder communication, budgeting, scheduling, and vendor management. * Oversee ongoing support, maintenance, and enhancement of application systems while ensuring adherence to ICT standards, policies, and best practices. * Contribute to business continuity, risk management, and operational planning activities. * Support organisational obligations relating to health, safety, quality, and customer service standards. * Undertake additional responsibilities as required to support business and ICT objectives. Experience \& Skills Required * Minimum of two years’ experience delivering business systems implementation projects, with demonstrated involvement across the full project lifecycle from initiation through to implementation and ongoing support. * Experience in one or more of the following areas: * Business Analysis * Application Development * IT Project Management * Application Testing * Strong communication, stakeholder engagement, and interpersonal skills. * Working knowledge of Microsoft SQL Server. * Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including experience with advanced functionality and macros. * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a dynamic and fast\-paced environment. * Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and organisational skills.

title: Architectural Graduate
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address 36 Lad Lane, Baggot St Lower, Dublin, D02 T208 Closing Date: 26 May 2026 Employer's Website https://www.hawkinsbrown.com/ Employer's Email NA Job Description At Hawkins\\Brown, we believe architecture should be serious about society and human at its core. As an employee‑owned, B Corp‑certified practice, we see business as a force for good \- designing places that strengthen communities, respond to the climate crisis, and leave the world better than we found it. We’re looking for talented and thoughtful Architectural Graduates to join our studios across London, Manchester, Edinburgh and Dublin. You’ll work within collaborative, interdisciplinary teams on a range of projects, contributing ideas, developing designs, and helping to shape meaningful, high‑quality architecture. Job Requirements * We’re interested in people who bring: * Strong design thinking and the ability to develop clear, well‑resolved ideas * Curiosity, initiative and a willingness to take ownership * A collaborative, open and generous approach to teamwork * Clear communication and the ability to engage with different perspectives * A genuine interest in the social and environmental impact of architecture Alongside this, you’ll have a solid grounding in the core tools and technical knowledge needed to contribute effectively to projects, including: * Ability to produce coherent architectural designs * A basic knowledge of building technologies, regulations and planning processes * Understanding of technical standards, regulatory frameworks and health \& safety requirements * Awareness of professional duty of care and project roles * Knowledge of CAD standards * Excellent 2D / 3D CAD and DTP skills * Experience in Revit What we offer In return, you’ll be part of a supportive, ambitious and inclusive culture \- one that invests in your development, values diverse perspectives, and gives you the opportunity to work on meaningful, high‑quality projects. As an employee‑owned practice, we work collectively, share success, and create an environment where everyone is encouraged to grow, contribute and thrive. Benefits: * 25 days annual leave, increasing to 28 with service * Duvet Day * Private Medical Insurance * Hybrid working and flexitime * Subsidised gym membership * Life Assurance and Income Protection * Interest\-free travel loan and rental deposit loan * Training and development programmes * Enhanced parental leave * Discretionary Profit Share Scheme Application Process To help us manage a high volume of applications and, more importantly, to give us the best chance of understanding you and your work, we ask that you include a maximum of three projects within your portfolio. These can be drawn from university, practice, or personal work; we’re more interested in the thinking behind the work than the setting. As part of the application, you’ll also be asked to respond to two questions. We appreciate that this requires extra time and consideration, but they are designed to help us get a clearer sense of your approach, interests and values, and to ensure we’re finding the right fit for both you and Hawkins\\Brown. Apply here *Hawkins\\Brown is an equal opportunities employer. We know that diverse teams are strong teams and welcome people of all identities, backgrounds and experiences to apply. We are committed to creating a fair, inclusive and accessible recruitment process. If you require any reasonable adjustments during the application or interview process, please let us know.*

title: Assistant Designer - Menswear
description: **Assistant Designer** Because your ideas are our style. Design, our way. As Assistant Designer, you will work closely alongside Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams to translate trend into product. Working alongside a Designer, you will collaborate with fellow product designers across the business you will create strong, fashion forward, commercial and global ranges that identify with the Primark brand. As Assistant Designer, you will demonstrate passion, curiosity and interest in fashion. You will predict key trends using the knowledge of top sellers, extensive research and the knowledge of forecasting tools. You will actively participate in key range building meetings and support product development through the completion of focused technical tasks. You will work within the development process, applying good knowledge of design tools, product qualities and specifications. You will be responsible for staying ahead of trends, continually research new ideas. **What You’ll Do As An Assistant Designer** We want you to feel challenged and inspired. Here, you’ll develop your skills across a range of responsibilities: * Research \& Spot Trends: Identify emerging fashion trends through research and translate them into commercially viable concepts for Primark’s ranges. * Support Range Development: Assist the Designer in building impactful, trend\-led product ranges that balance creativity and commerciality. * Collaborate Across Teams: Work closely with Designers, Buyers, and Merchandisers to ensure creative vision is executed and aligned with brand strategy. * Manage Design Process: Track design updates, maintain critical path timelines, and prepare trend and design files for presentations and supplier meetings. * Analyse Performance: Review past range performance with Buying and Merchandising teams to inform future design decisions. * Ensure Commercial Awareness: Understand cost implications, competitor activity, and how trends influence sales to support profitable product development. **Key Interactions and Engagements as part of this role:** As Assistant Designer you will report directly to a Designer. You will work closely with Primark’s Buying and Merchandising teams, as well as collaborating with Design teams across all product categories to ensure alignment and a unified Design vision across our brand. You will also be expected to represent your product categories on working groups and forums across departmentally **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. Let’s talk lifestyle. Let’s talk lifestyle: 27 days of leave, plus bank holidays and if you want, you can buy 5 more. Because Primark is all about tailoring to you, we offer Tax Saver Tickets, fitness centre, and a subsidised cafeteria. **What You’ll Bring** We want every person in our organisation to feel like they’re making a key contribution. Here are some of the experiences and skills you’ll need for the role: * Design Expertise: At least 2 years’ experience in a Trainee or Assistant Designer role, with a solid understanding of fashion design principles. * Relevant Qualification: Degree in fashion design or equivalent practical experience. * Technical Proficiency: Strong skills in Adobe Illustrator and Photoshop to create high\-quality design assets. * Commercial Mindset: Good business acumen and genuine interest in retail, with the ability to balance creativity and commerciality. * Attention to Detail \& Organization: Accuracy, proactive approach, and excellent planning skills to manage deadlines effectively. * Collaborative \& Creative Attitude: Strong communication skills, positive energy, and an innovative mindset to thrive in a fast\-paced environment. Excited? Good. Because it’s energising to put your skills to work, in a growing global business committed to encouraging people to express themselves – and feel a sense of belonging. Apply to be part of Primark’s future, today… and enjoy career growth, our way. **About Primark** Primark is an international clothing retailer employing more than 70,000 colleagues across 16 countries in Europe and the US. Founded in Ireland in 1969 under the Penneys brand, Primark aims to create maximum joy at minimum cost – less money and less impact on the planet. Primark continues to expand across new and existing markets with the aim of reaching 530 stores by the end of 2026\. Every person impacts the culture at Primark. Our values: Caring (we always strive to put people first), Dynamic (we bravely push the boundaries to stay ahead) and Together (we learn more, laugh more and achieve more as a team) outline the behaviours we expect of our people as we work with each other, our customers, our suppliers and our partners. Let’s see what impact you can make! **REQ ID:** JR\-3870

title: Assistant Golf Professional - PGA
description: At The K Club, we are currently seeking an **Assistant Golf Professional** to join our South Golf Team for a very busy season ahead. Join us for an exciting few years ahead as we are set to host the Women's Irish Open in 2026 and the Men's Irish Open on our Palmer North Course in 2027\. Trainees and qualified professionals are all welcome to get in touch! **A UNIQUE RESORT** The K Club is a truly captivating leisure destination tucked away in lush Irish countryside \- yet on Dublin's doorstep. We are a unique resort, a renowned place of celebration, with a spirited history that spans hundreds of years. We're a 5 star destination which prides itself on having an incredible 5 star team to match. Across our 550 acre estate, we offer a tremendous variety of career opportunities for those who are looking to become part of team that strives to help one another shine. The K Club is an industry trailblazer. We welcome innovative thinking, bold ideas and insights garnered from your own experience. We're certified as a Great Place to Work by our very own employees. That's because our hardworking team members feel listened to, encouraged and appreciated. If this sounds like the place where you could begin the next exciting chapter in your career, let's talk.\# **Here's how you'll bring our trailblazing vision to life:** * A fantastic opportunity for someone looking to be part of a 5\*, world renowned Resort \& Golf Course * Opportunity to work at the Arnold Palmer designed Palmer South golf course * A heavy involvement in running the Pro Shop and serving members and visitors alike * Opportunity to coach and custom fit at our new Trackman Driving range, opening soon * Engage with an active membership **This is how we see you:** * A trainee or qualified PGA Professional * Will be flexible in their working hours **What's on offer?** * Ongoing investment in your personal development with access to internal and external training programmes. * Great opportunities to progress your career. * Monthly recognition programme. * Refer a Friend Scheme. * Employee Assistance Programme. * Social events. * Uniform. * Meals while on duty. * Discount on stays at the resort and on food \& beverage for you and your family and friends. * Discount on use of our golf courses. * Life Assurance. * Monthly Service Charge payment * Bike to Work Scheme * Discounted local gym membership **Data Retention** Please note that all applications and CV's submitted for a role at The K Club will be kept on file for a period of 12 months. Should you not wish for your data to be held following the closure of the application process then we would ask that you please make note of this when submitting your application. The Human Resources Team The K Club

title: Associate (Tunnel)
description: **Associate (Tunnels and Underground Structures)** **Dublin** **Permanent** **Office/Home Hybrid Working** **Do you want to help grow a team in Ireland? Can you design a sustainable future? Are you a chartered civil engineer? Then, shape a sustainable and liveable world with us.** **Create Solutions that will enable a better tomorrow** To support the drive towards innovation and the development of new sustainable solutions, we are looking for an Associate to join us. Working on Tunnels and Underground Structures, you’ll act as a design lead for specific components of your projects, liaising directly with clients and the broader design team. You’ll also oversee and guide more junior staff in the design of infrastructure components. Recent projects have included: High Speed Rail 2, Second Avenue Subway (New York), Sizewell C nuclear power station, hydro storage scheme (Scotland), Network Rail rehabilitation of historic tunnels, Cross Island Link (Singapore), major road tunnels, and bids for small, medium and large tunnelling projects. Your key responsibilities will be: * Coordinating the delivery of your projects to time and budget, ensuring a high standard of technical quality and compliance with our internal management systems * Production of engineering design, assessment or checking work including preparation or checking of calculations, written reports and other contract deliverables * Developing and mentoring less experienced engineers and technicians within your project teams, working with line managers to maximise training opportunities * Maintaining and strengthening client relationships through your project work, including identifying and realising any potential business opportunities * Assisting with the preparation of bids and tenders and supporting the Directors in pursuing business development opportunities if required. **Your skills. Our team. Together we design the future.** The first step to success in this role is that you are eager to collaborate with the people around you, whether they are colleagues, partners, or customers. Developing ties with others is something you do by acting respectfully and delivering on your promises. And you never get set in your ways but keep exploring new insights and ways to improve. On top of that, you’ll: * Be a chartered civil engineer * Have significant experience in a design environment working on tunnels, heavy civil, or other complex buried structures in the UK or internationally * Demonstrate the ability to lead project teams and oversee the delivery of projects, either as client's or contractor's designer, ideally both * Knowledge of soil structure interaction principles and their application in both structural analysis (Lusas, Strand7, SAP 2000\) and geotechnical analysis (Plaxis software) is preferrable. * A proven record in the design of bored tunnels, mined excavations, stations, basements, underground structures, temporary works * Demonstrate knowledge across a range of methods of tunnelling (small and large diameters) and underground structure construction methods. * Be an accomplished team leader with the ability to support, mentor and develop less experienced engineers * Have a sound understanding and experience in the application of the CDM Regulations **A place to work and so much more** At COWI, we work together with our customers to shape a sustainable and liveable world. We do it by applying our knowledge and curiosity – and sometimes even our courage – to create the solutions the world needs today to enable a better tomorrow. We support our customers’ transition towards increased sustainability. We strive to lead by example by embedding environmental management, social responsibility and good governance into our business practices and the options we provide to our customers. We value differences and development and cultivate an environment of belonging and having fun. Because that is what brings out the best in you, at work and at home.With offices primarily located in the Nordic region, the UK, North America and India, we are currently more than 7,500 people who bring their expertise in engineering, architecture, energy and environment into play. **What we also offer:** To us, a fair reward is more than a competitive salary. We want to take care of our people professionally and personally as well. Some of our benefits include: * 28 days' holiday plus bank and public holidays * Formalised development plans and yearly promotion opportunities * Private health care * Flexible and hybrid working conditions * Annual subscription fees paid for your membership of two Professional Institutions * Pension scheme * Life assurance **Got more questions?** If you have any unanswered questions about the role or what we have to offer, please reach out to Laura Dobson LADN@cowi.com Get to know us even better at our website, www.cowi.com, where you can learn more about our projects, our strategy, what we want to achieve and what life is like at COWI. **Equal opportunity employer.** COWI provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to ethnicity, colour, religion, gender, national origin, age or disability. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

title: Associate - Civil Engineering
description: **Associate \- Civil Engineering** ================================== ### ***Dublin \| Senior Leadership Role \| Strategic Impact*** CS Consulting is seeking a highly experienced Civil Engineer to join our Dublin\-based leadership team. This is a senior technical and managerial role offering the opportunity to take ownership for the delivery of civil infrastructure on projects while contributing directly to the strategic growth and performance of the business. **Upcoming projects include:** * Large\-scale residential projects for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and their Development Partners * Private residential projects (high\-rise and low\-rise) nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and public/ private sector Clients * New\-build commercial office and hotel projects * Commercial office and hotel refurbishment and fit\-out projects * Industrial and data centre projects **The Role** This is a senior, high\-impact engineering role with significant influence across projects and teams. You will lead the design and delivery of civil infrastructure across major projects spanning multiple sectors, combining technical authority with strong commercial awareness. You will manage projects from early concept through to construction and handover, working closely with Directors, clients, and multidisciplinary teams. This role carries significant responsibility for engineering decisions, programme delivery, cost control, and client relationships. You will also play a key role in mentoring and developing engineers within the practice, while representing CS Consulting at a senior professional level. **Candidate Profile** * Masters or Honours degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Chartered Engineer status with Engineers Ireland and/or ICE * At least 10\+ years’ post\-qualification experience in a consulting engineering environment * Proven experience leading the delivery of all civil infrastructure on a broad range of project types with significant responsibility and strong conceptual, detailed design and construction stage abilities * Experience ensuring designs are developed in accordance with relevant Irish standards, including Uisce Éireann publications, the Greater Dublin Regional Code of Practice for Drainage Works, and Local Authority guidelines * Detailed knowledge of the Irish planning system, typical planning reports, and experience liaising directly with Local Authorities and Statutory Bodies/ Utility Providers such as Uisce Eireann on behalf of Clients at pre\-planning, planning and compliance stages * Experience working with Contractors during the delivery stages of projects including carrying our BCAR inspections to ensure works are completed in accordance with designs, addressing submittals and RFI’s, attending site meetings, closing out snags, etc. * Proficiency using civil design software such as InfoDrainage, Civil 3D, AutoCAD, etc. (or equivalent) * Working knowledge of AutoCAD/ Revit in a coordinated BIM environment. * High level of competence in Microsoft Office and project planning tools * Demonstrated ability to exercise independent professional judgement on complex technical and commercial matters * Strong leadership and people\-management skills within multidisciplinary teams * Sound commercial awareness with experience managing project finances and client relationships * Adaptable, solutions\-focused mindset with a proactive and professional approach * Full Drivers Licence desirable **What We Offer** * Competitive Salary: We offer a competitive base salary and defined bonus scheme, in addition to a generous annual leave allowance \+ company bonus day, and employer pension contribution. * Mid\-Size Firm with Large\-Scale Projects: Opportunity to take ownership of your work with the support of highly experienced Senior Engineers and Directors as well as a talented team of engineering and technician resources. * Broad Range of Projects with the Best Clients: We work across commercial, residential, healthcare, education, hotel, leisure and retail with Ireland's top public and private Clients including the Land Development Agency (LDA), Ronan Group Real Estate, McGarrell Reilly, Quintain, Westridge RE, DRES, Hibernia Real Estate Group, Bartra, Tetrarch, Local Authorities, University of Limerick, Ireland's Top Contractors, and many more. * Shape the way we work: Our Organisation builds on energetic colleagues with a desire to learn, contribute their experience and improve our way of working. We believe in innovation and improvement and utilise data analysis to learn and grow. * CPD Learning: Engineers Ireland approved CPD programme and regular lessons learned and knowledge sharing sessions in a round\-table format. * Leading Software Tech: Access to leading technical software and review our requirements regularly to ensure we are using the best and latest in technology.

title: Associate Engineer
description: 🌍Job Title: Associate/Principal Engineer – Water Division: Water Engineering Location: Dublin/Cork, Ireland **💡About Us** Ayesa Engineering is a global engineering consultancy with over 13,000 employees, where our UK \& Ireland business combines international strength with the agility and autonomy of an SME. With a presence in 40 countries, Ayesa Engineering has built a strong international reputation across a diverse range of engineering disciplines. Our expertise includes water and flood infrastructure, transportation, marine and coastal engineering, geoscience, environmental services, energy, and building structures. Our UK \& Ireland teams deliver technically challenging, varied projects that develop broad expertise without confining people to narrow roles — offering the opportunity to make a meaningful local impact while benefiting from the stability, knowledge, and career opportunities of a well\-established global organisation. **What You Will Be Doing:** As an Associate/Principal Engineer, you’ll lead and deliver Wastewater and Water design packages across diverse sectors including commercial, residential, education, healthcare, and public infrastructure. We require a self\-driving engineer at senior level to manage predominantly UÉ related work but also to support on our civil designs for contractors. **Key Responsibilities** As the technical lead, you will ensure all designs meet the specific Standard Details and Codes of Practice set by Uisce Éireann or other relevant authority. * Design Lead: Spearhead the design of water distribution networks, foul sewerage collection systems, pumping stations and general civil engineering. * Hydraulic Modelling: Oversee or perform complex hydraulic analysis for water and wastewater networks using software packages. * Flood Risk: Prepare and review Site\-Specific Flood Risk Assessments in accordance with "The Planning System and Flood Risk Management" guidelines. * Understanding the Irish planning landscape is critical for moving a project from concept to construction. Understanding of the Irish property acquisition and wayleave process. * Planning Submissions: Prepare technical reports and drawings for Part 8 (Local Authority) or Strategic Infrastructure Development (SID) planning applications. * Statutory Liaising: Negotiate with Local Authorities, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), and Uisce Éireann for connections and/or design diversions. * Environmental Coordination: Collaborate with ecologists / hydrologists / archaeologists to ensure designs comply with Appropriate Assessments (AA) and Environmental Impact Assessment Reports (EIAR). * Contract Documentation: Draft tender documents using the Capital Works Management Framework (CWMF) for public projects or FIDIC/NEC4 for private/utility contracts. * Contract Administration: Act as the "Employer’s Representative" managing claims, variations, and payment certificates. * Procurement: Evaluate tenders and provide technical recommendations to clients for contractor selection. * You will be responsible to work in multidisciplinary teams while ensuring legal safety compliance. * PSDP Role: Fulfil the duties of the Project Supervisor for the Design Process (PSDP) under the 2006 Safety, Health and Welfare at Work (Construction) Regulations. * Financial Oversight: Monitor project budgets, resource allocation (junior engineers / technicians), and ensure the "man\-hour" targets are met. * Quality Assurance: Implement and maintain ISO quality standards for all deliverables, ensuring "Right First Time" submissions to clients. * Site Inspections: Conduct surveys and oversee site investigation (SI) works to inform the design phase. * Snagging \& Handover: Manage the final commissioning of water infrastructure, ensuring all "As\-Built" drawings and O\&M manuals are accepted by the end\-user. * Management of relationships with key clients and contacts across the business including client liaison and development. * Management of projects within time, cost and quality constraints and standards * Management of the day\-to\-day work of the team. * Champions and promotes professional advice, guidance and support across the business. * Monitors and manages compliance and KPI’s * Estimating, pricing and delivering against work targets. * Management of engineering outsource partners. **What You Bring:** * Exposure to some or all of the following: drinking water treatment and distribution, wastewater collection and treatment, drainage and flood risk management and assessment. * General Civil Engineering Design/Water infrastructure design experience. * Ability to plan, organise and manage resources, provide leadership, mentor and motivate a team of Engineers. * Can effectively communicate with clients, colleagues, external agencies and build effective working relationships. * Commercially astute, prepare technical and commercial bids, win work, project manage and consistently deliver significant projects to program and budget. * Take ownership of multiple projects managing risk, ensuring financial, commercial and contractual compliance. * Able to promote and champion a quality culture to achieve company goals, is committed to Continued Professional Development and keeps up to date with current trends in the industry. * Proven experience in Project Management of large Water Infrastructure Projects. * Experience of contract administration, procurement procedures and contract forms an advantage. * Experience as an External Service Provider (ESP) with Uisce Éireann will be a distinct advantage. * Degree qualified in relevant field * Relevant professional qualification: Engineering/Project Management * Minimum of 10 to 15 years relevant experience * Chartered status or equivalent qualification. * Knowledge of local legislation and its application a benefit. **What We Offer:** We offer a competitive salary package along with a comprehensive range of benefits designed to support your professional growth and enhance your overall well\-being: * Competitive Salary: We offer a competitive salary package to attract and retain top talent in the industry. * Flexible Working: Enjoy flexible working hours and hybrid working, allowing you to achieve a better work\-life balance. * Employer Pension Match Scheme: We offer a pension match scheme to help you secure your financial future. * Income Protection: Receive support in safeguarding your income in the event of illness or injury. * Life Assurance: Gain peace of mind with life assurance coverage. * Training and Development Programme: Access a range of training and development opportunities to enhance your skills and advance your career. * Family \& Well\-Being Support: Benefit from our Enhanced Maternity \& Paternity Benefits and our confidential support services to address personal or professional challenges, included in our Employee Assistance Programme. * Sports and Social: Engage in our vibrant sports and social activities to foster a sense of community and well\-being among our team members. * Increased Annual Leave: 25 days’ annual leave plus statutory bank holidays, enjoy increased annual leave entitlements based on years of service. * Recognition Awards: Receive recognition for your contributions through our awards program, celebrating achievements and excellence. We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation

title: Associate Representative - Inside Sales
description: **Job Description** As an Associate Representative \- Inside Sales, you will interact with existing customers, primarily by phone and email, to increase sales of the Company's products and/or services by obtaining and up\-selling orders, creating customer satisfaction and adding value to the customer's buying experience. You will collaborate with outside sales to ensure goals are being met. **Responsibilities** * Obtain orders through email and telephone calls, verifies and enters items, transfers orders to fulfillment, explains stock\-outs and expected delivery dates. * Increase orders by suggesting related items, explaining features, and checks customer’s buying history. * With guidance, owns, qualifies and develops opportunities passed from marketing, outside sales, and national accounts. * Prepare, generate, and follow up on verbal or written quotations to secure orders, or determine reason for loss of order. * Review open customer order reports and act on open items, including those items that may be at risk in meeting customer’s promised delivery date. * Maintain distribution system backorder report, associated customer expediting report and notices, and customer notification. * Report industry trends, competitive pricing and customer feedback to management. **Qualifications** * High School Degree or Equivalent required; Bachelor’s degree preferred. * Preferred knowledge and experience in sales and sales administration; still acquiring higher level knowledge and skills, however still a productive professional contributor (0\-2 years). * Familiar with Microsoft Office, and ability to perform basic computer skills * Ability to perform multiple tasks simultaneously * Ability to work in a team environment * Ability to communicate clearly, both verbally and in written form * Attention to detail * Ability to prospect and market concepts to existing and potentially new accounts * Ability to act and solve routine or standard problems * Ability to identify and define problems and possible solutions independently; chooses among existing solutions * Ability to work independently with supervision * Ability to travel up to 25% **About The Team** At Wesco, we build, connect, power and protect the world. As a leading provider of business\-to\-business distribution, logistics services and supply chain solutions, we create a world that you can depend on. Our Company’s greatest asset is our people. Wesco is committed to fostering a workplace where every individual is respected, valued, and empowered to succeed. We promote a culture that is grounded in teamwork and respect. With a workforce of over 20,000 people worldwide, we embrace the unique perspectives each person brings. Through comprehensive benefits and active community engagement, we create an environment where every team member has the opportunity to thrive. Learn more about Working at Wesco here and apply online today! Founded in 1922 and headquartered in Pittsburgh, Wesco is a publicly traded (NYSE: WCC) FORTUNE 500® company. *Wesco International, Inc., including its subsidiaries and affiliates (“Wesco”) provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, religion, color, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, or other characteristics protected by law. US applicants only, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer.* *Los Angeles Unincorporated County Candidates Only: Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance and the California Fair Chance Act.* *This posting is for a current, active vacancy intended for immediate hire.*

title: Associate Systems Administrator
description: **Who We Are** At CarGurus (NASDAQ: CARG), our mission is to give people the power to reach their destination. We started as a small team of developers determined to bring trust and transparency to car shopping. Since then, our history of innovation and go\-to\-market acceleration has driven industry\-leading growth. In fact, we’re the largest and fastest\-growing automotive marketplace, and we’ve been profitable for over 15 years. **What We Do** The market is evolving, and we are too, moving the entire automotive journey online and guiding our customers through every step. That includes everything from the sale of an old car to the financing, purchase, and delivery of a new one. Today, tens of millions of consumers visit CarGurus.com each month, and \~30,000 dealerships use our products. But they're not the only ones who love CarGurus—our employees do, too. We have a people\-first culture that fosters kindness, collaboration, and innovation, and empowers our Gurus with tools to fuel their career growth. Disrupting a trillion\-dollar industry requires fresh and diverse perspectives. Come join us for the ride! **Role Overview** The Associate Systems Administrator is an entry\-level position within the IT Systems team based in our Dublin office. This role serves as a critical bridge between our IT Service Desk and Global Systems functions, primarily focusing on supporting our EU and UK\-based employees. You will work closely with the IT Service Desk team to resolve technical escalations while aligning with US\-based systems engineers to maintain global applications, security policies, patching standards, and network infrastructure across our European locations. This role requires a blend of operational support, technical curiosity, and a commitment to maintaining a secure, highly available environment. On Call work may be required with this roll as needed by the business. In addition to the responsibilities above, this role also requires regular physical activity, including moving around the office to assist with IT or AV event related tasks, lifting and carrying equipment (upwards of 50lbs), moving tables and chairs, and plugging in devices under desks and other confined spaces. The Associate Systems Administrator must be able to perform these duties safely and efficiently as part of their responsibilities. **What You'll Do** **System Administration \& Support** * Perform daily monitoring, management, and troubleshooting for core corporate enterprise software platforms. * Assist with the configuration, deployment, and maintenance of identity platforms and collaboration tools. * Follow established change management procedures for system updates and modifications. * Maintain accurate system documentation, including runbooks and knowledge base articles for the Service Desk. **Workspace \& Endpoint Management** * Support the administration of Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions such as Jamf and Microsoft Intune. * Assist in managing end\-user devices, including software deployments, VDI deployment and desktop configurations. * Help implement and enforce security measures like software patches and OS updates. **Networking \& Infrastructure** * Provide first\-level support for network escalations, including Wi\-Fi, VPN, and firewall issues. * Assist with basic network troubleshooting and performance monitoring for EU office environments. * Support the setup and maintenance of local network hardware under senior guidance. **Identity \& Security Compliance** * Manage user lifecycles within identity platforms (e.g., Okta) to ensure appropriate access controls. * Contribute to incident response efforts by participating in a regional on\-call rotation. * Review incoming IT requests to ensure alignment with company Information Security and governance policies. **What You'll Bring** **Required Skills \& Experience** * Foundational experience with Identity and Access Management (IAM) platforms such as Okta or OneLogin. * Proficiency working within enterprise ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow, Atlassian/Jira). * Experience with enterprise collaboration tools including Google Workspace, Microsoft O365, and Slack. * Exposure to MDM tools such as Microsoft Intune or Jamf. * Basic understanding of enterprise networking principles and troubleshooting techniques.Demonstrated ability to document technical processes and follow standard operating procedures. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with Audiovisual (AV) setup and troubleshooting for conference rooms. * Basic administration experience with enterprise cloud\-based telephony systems (e.g., Dialpad). * A background in Systems Administration (min 2 years experience) and some experience with IT support or service desk operations. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across time zones. The displayed range represents the expected annual base salary / On\-Target Earnings (OTE) for this position. On\-Target Earnings (OTE) is inclusive of base salary and on\-target commission earnings, which applies exclusively to sales roles. Individual pay within this range is determined by work location and other factors such as job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. This annual base salary forms part of a comprehensive Total Rewards Package. In addition to benefits, this role may qualify for discretionary bonuses/incentives and Restricted Stock Units (RSUs). Position Pay Range €38\.000—€48\.000 EUR **Working at CarGurus** We reward our Gurus’ curiosity and passion with best\-in\-class benefits and compensation, including equity for all employees, both when they start and as they continue to grow with us.
