title: Associate Systems Administrator
description: **Who we are** At CarGurus (NASDAQ: CARG), our mission is to give people the power to reach their destination. We started as a small team of developers determined to bring trust and transparency to car shopping. Since then, our history of innovation and go\-to\-market acceleration has driven industry\-leading growth. In fact, we're the largest and fastest\-growing automotive marketplace, and we've been profitable for over 15 years. **What we do** The market is evolving, and we are too, moving the entire automotive journey online and guiding our customers through every step. That includes everything from the sale of an old car to the financing, purchase, and delivery of a new one. Today, tens of millions of consumers visit CarGurus.com each month, and \~30,000 dealerships use our products. But they're not the only ones who love CarGurus—our employees do, too. We have a people\-first culture that fosters kindness, collaboration, and innovation, and empowers our Gurus with tools to fuel their career growth. Disrupting a trillion\-dollar industry requires fresh and diverse perspectives. Come join us for the ride! **Role overview** The Associate Systems Administrator is an entry\-level position within the IT Systems team based in our Dublin office. This role serves as a critical bridge between our IT Service Desk and Global Systems functions, primarily focusing on supporting our EU and UK\-based employees. You will work closely with the IT Service Desk team to resolve technical escalations while aligning with US\-based systems engineers to maintain global applications, security policies, patching standards, and network infrastructure across our European locations. This role requires a blend of operational support, technical curiosity, and a commitment to maintaining a secure, highly available environment. On Call work may be required with this roll as needed by the business. In addition to the responsibilities above, this role also requires regular physical activity, including moving around the office to assist with IT or AV event related tasks, lifting and carrying equipment (upwards of 50lbs), moving tables and chairs, and plugging in devices under desks and other confined spaces. The Associate Systems Administrator must be able to perform these duties safely and efficiently as part of their responsibilities. **What you'll do** **System Administration \& Support** * Perform daily monitoring, management, and troubleshooting for core corporate enterprise software platforms. * Assist with the configuration, deployment, and maintenance of identity platforms and collaboration tools. * Follow established change management procedures for system updates and modifications. * Maintain accurate system documentation, including runbooks and knowledge base articles for the Service Desk. **Workspace \& Endpoint Management** * Support the administration of Mobile Device Management (MDM) solutions such as Jamf and Microsoft Intune. * Assist in managing end\-user devices, including software deployments, VDI deployment and desktop configurations. * Help implement and enforce security measures like software patches and OS updates. **Networking \& Infrastructure** * Provide first\-level support for network escalations, including Wi\-Fi, VPN, and firewall issues. * Assist with basic network troubleshooting and performance monitoring for EU office environments. * Support the setup and maintenance of local network hardware under senior guidance. **Identity \& Security Compliance** * Manage user lifecycles within identity platforms (e.g., Okta) to ensure appropriate access controls. * Contribute to incident response efforts by participating in a regional on\-call rotation. * Review incoming IT requests to ensure alignment with company Information Security and governance policies. **What you'll bring** **Required Skills \& Experience** * Foundational experience with Identity and Access Management (IAM) platforms such as Okta or OneLogin. * Proficiency working within enterprise ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow, Atlassian/Jira). * Experience with enterprise collaboration tools including Google Workspace, Microsoft O365, and Slack. * Exposure to MDM tools such as Microsoft Intune or Jamf. * Basic understanding of enterprise networking principles and troubleshooting techniques.Demonstrated ability to document technical processes and follow standard operating procedures. **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with Audiovisual (AV) setup and troubleshooting for conference rooms. * Basic administration experience with enterprise cloud\-based telephony systems (e.g., Dialpad). * A background in Systems Administration (min 2 years experience) and some experience with IT support or service desk operations. * Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across time zones. **Working at CarGurus** We reward our Gurus' curiosity and passion with best\-in\-class benefits and compensation, including equity for all employees, both when they start and as they continue to grow with us. Our career development and corporate giving programs, as well as our employee resource groups (ERGs) and communities, help people build connections while making an impact in personally meaningful ways. A flexible hybrid model and robust time off policies encourage work\-life balance and individual well\-being. Thoughtful perks like daily free lunch, a new car discount, meditation and fitness apps, commuting cost coverage, and more help our people create space for what matters most in their personal and professional lives. **We welcome all** CarGurus strives to be a place to which people can bring the ultimate expression of themselves and their potential—starting with our hiring process. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation. We foster an inclusive environment that values people for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. That's why we hope you'll apply even if you don't check every box listed in the job description. title: Associate, Operations
description: The Opportunity As a member of the Dublin Operations team, you will support the end\-to\-end trade lifecycle for Nearwater’s MiFID Regulated European business, with a primary focus on Total Return Swaps (TRS) and equity\-linked financing. You will be a day\-to\-day operational point of contact for clients, counterparties, and internal stakeholders across Origination, Trading, Middle Office, Finance, and Technology, contributing to scalable processes and controls as the Dublin office continues to grow. What You’ll Do **Support the trade lifecycle** * Support the end\-to\-end operational lifecycle for TRS and equity\-linked products, including resets, dividends, corporate actions, financing legs, collateral, and unwinds * Develop working knowledge of TRS documentation (ISDA/CSA), margin and collateral workflows, and equity swap mechanics * Support end\-to\-end operational processes for Repo transactions and LSTA/LMA loans alongside the core TRS book * Investigate and resolve reconciliation items, ensuring completeness and accuracy of books and records * Escalate settlement fails, outstanding margin calls, and disputes in a timely and transparent manner * Execute the daily operations controls and the End of Day process **Contribute to best\-in\-class processes** * Contribute to process design, automation, and the build\-out of the Dublin operations function * Maintain a strong control mindset, continuously monitoring processes and escalating potential issues * Support compliance with industry regulations and standards in relation to derivative transactions **Partner across the firm and externally** * Act as a day\-to\-day operational point of contact for client queries, ensuring timely, accurate resolution and a high\-quality client experience * Support Origination and Trading on new product and new client onboarding, including operational readiness for TRS structures * Liaise directly with our Custodian and Prime Broker on day\-to\-day operational matters * Contribute to KPI / KRI reporting and governance and management review materials * Provide partnership with colleagues in our New York, Toronto and London offices What We’re Looking Fpr **Experience and expertise** * 3–5\+ years of experience in operations, middle\-office, or trade support, with meaningful time spent on TRS and Equity Swap products at a bank, prime broker, hedge fund, or similar firms * Working knowledge of the TRS lifecycle, resets, dividend pass\-throughs, corporate actions, financing accruals, collateral / IM / VM, and termination mechanics * Working knowledge of TRS and equity swaps; exposure to Repo and LSTA/LMA loan products * Familiarity with ISDA Master Agreements, CSAs, and confirmation processes for equity TRS * Exposure to process re\-engineering, control build, and partnering with Technology on operational tooling * Strong experience with reconciliation processes, trade lifecycle management, and exposure/margin workflows * Hands\-on experience with Cash and Equity Swaps, Loans Administration, and EMIR / MiFID reporting required * Experience working in a MiFID investment firm preferred * Working knowledge of financial markets and industry technology including AcadiaSoft, ClearPar, GLAS, and Bloomberg * Proactive, initiative\-taking individual who can effectively manage multiple priorities and operate through ambiguity * Ability to thrive in a structured, high\-volume, high\-risk, time\-sensitive, and demanding environment * Excellent verbal and written communication, with confidence in client and counterparty calls * Strong technical aptitude, including high proficiency with advanced Excel and the ability to adopt new systems and tools * Capable of collaborating effectively within a team and excelling when working independently What You’ll Get * Opportunity to help build the Dublin operations function, with direct exposure to firm leadership in New York and London * Joining a firm in the early stage of its evolution in Dublin, with meaningful impact on the company’s growth and customer success * A platform to grow your operational career as the Dublin office scales * Inclusive workplace that values diversity and ensures everyone has a voice * Collaborative, supportive culture where new ideas are encouraged and contributions are recognised * Exposure to complex, high\-impact work, giving you hands\-on experience across the wider Dublin teams (Risk, Finance, and Trading) that you simply don’t get in larger organisations * A fast\-moving, intellectually challenging environment where continuous learning is part of everyday life * A collaborative culture where we work hard, support each other, and enjoy what we do * A competitive compensation and benefits package designed to reward your contribution and growth How You Work * You take ownership and operate with a strong control mindset * You’re detail\-oriented and accurate, with a high bar for books and records * You’re proactive and initiative\-taking, comfortable managing multiple priorities and operating through ambiguity * You collaborate effectively within a team and excel when working independently * You communicate clearly with clients, counterparties, and internal stakeholders at all levels * You thrive in a structured, high\-volume, high\-risk, time\-sensitive, and demanding environment Who We Are Nearwater Capital is a rapidly growing and innovative specialty finance company headquartered in New York City with a global presence and client base. Established in 2017, Nearwater has distinguished itself as a leader in delivering thoughtful, solutions\-based financing to some of the largest financial institutions and financial services firms in the world. Nearwater is expanding its business in Europe and has established an office in Dublin. Nearwater Capital Markets Europe (NCME) is authorised by the Central Bank of Ireland (CBI) as a MiFID investment firm. Nearwater offers an attractive market compensation and benefits package. As a creative, intellectual capital firm, Nearwater is proud to be 100% work from office.

title: BIM Graduate
description: **Job Summary** Our client is an established construction company with operations across Ireland, the UK, and Northern Europe. They work across sectors such as Data Centres, Life Sciences, Aviation, High\-Value Manufacturing, and Critical Logistics, delivering complex, high\-performance projects. We are seeking a talented BIM Graduate to join their high\-performance operations department in Dublin. The role will involve close collaboration with commercial teams, contracts managers, clients, design teams, and project directors. **Reporting To** BIM Lead **Role Responsibilities** * Produce detailed 3D BIM models in line with specific project requirements * Ensure models align with standards and goals set in the BIM Execution Plan and BIM workflows * Collaborate internally and externally with other disciplines * Create BIM modelling content, including setting up additional Revit families and templates * Develop construction drawings and extract data * Maintain and administer the Common Data Environment (CDE) * Carry out checks on Revit models * Liaise with subcontractors and design teams to agree on objectives - **Requirements** * Degree in Building Information Modelling graduating in Summer 2026 (Level 7/8\) or a related Engineering/Architecture Undergraduate Degree (Level 7/8\) (preferable) * Internship or work experience in a BIM field (preferred) * Familiarity with AutoCAD, Revit, and Navisworks * Proficient in Microsoft Office * Some understanding of BIM procedures, protocols, and data management software tools * Strong communication skills * Ability to collaborate with various stakeholders * Eagerness to learn, passion for digitalising common construction practices, and keen desire to influence the use of innovative technology within a business * Self\-starter who exercises good judgement under pressure, patient, and flexible **Package** A competitive remuneration package including basic salary, pension, life assurance, and healthcare. **Career Path** Graduate → Technician → Coordinator → Lead → Manager *Please note we cannot offer any sponsorships*

title: BIM Technician - Data Centres
description: **About The Job** The BIM Technician is responsible for producing coordinated, high\-quality Revit models and drawings on data centre projects, working as part of a wider BIM and Engineering team. This role focuses on MEP/M\&E modelling, drawing production, and clash coordination in support of the BIM Project Lead and Engineering teams. The BIM Technician plays a hands\-on role in day\-to\-day model production, ensuring deliverables are accurate, compliant, and aligned with project requirements. **About You** **Required Skills \& Experience** * Experience in BIM delivery within MEP or multidisciplinary projects. * Experience working on large\-scale or complex projects (e.g. data centres). * Advanced proficiency in Revit MEP (essential). * Experience in clash detection tools (e.g. Navisworks). * Understanding of BIM coordination processes and workflows. * Experience leading or coordinating BIM teams on projects. * Excellent communication and coordination skills. * Familiarity with common data environments (e.g. ACC/FORMA). **Desirable** * Knowledge of BIM standards and QA processes. * Understanding of ISO 19650 principles. * Knowledge of industry Revit add\-ins to streamline processes. **Success Measures** * Models are fully coordinated with minimal clashes at issue stage. * Drawings are accurate, consistent, and compliant. * BIM deliverables meet programme deadlines. * Effective communication between BIM and Engineering teams. * High model quality and adherence to standards. ****Key Responsibilities**** **Project BIM Delivery** * Ensure all BIM deliverables meet project programme and milestones. * Monitor modelling progress and report status to the BIM Project Manager. **Model Coordination \& Quality** * Develop and maintain coordinated Revit models with other disciplines. * Run clash detection processes and coordinate issue resolution. * Identify clashes and communicate them clearly to Engineering Teams for resolution. * Ensure models are structured, efficient, and aligned with best practice. **Standard Compliance** * Ensure all models comply with internal BIM standards and QA processes. * Implement client\-specific BIM requirements on projects. * Maintain model integrity, naming conventions, and data consistency. * Ensure drawings extracted from models meet company quality standards. * Setup and develop Models in line with Client BEP requirements. **Drawing Production \& Output** * Oversee the production of drawings from Revit models. * Ensure drawings are accurate, coordinated, and ready for issue. * Maintain consistency across all project outputs. **Stakeholder Coordination** * Represent Ethos in BIM coordination meetings. * Collaborate with Engineering Teams to align BIM models with design intent. * Coordinate with external consultants and project stakeholders. * Communicate BIM\-related issues, risks, and progress effectively. **Model Health \& Performance** * Monitor model performance, file size, and usability. * Ensure models remain clean, efficient, and suitable for project use. * Implement best practices to avoid model degradation over time. **Key Objectives** * Deliver coordinated, high\-quality BIM models and drawings. * Ensure efficient and proactive coordination across disciplines. * Maintain consistency with BIM standards and project requirements. * Support predictable and reliable BIM delivery on projects. **Authority \& Decision Making** * Responsible for BIM coordination and delivery at project level. * Oversees modelling teams and day\-to\-day BIM activities. * Escalates technical or resource issues to the BIM Project Manager when required. **What This Role Does NOT Include** * Engineering design decisions or approvals. * Responsibility for system layouts or technical calculations. * Issuing models externally without required approvals. * Changing BIM standards independently. * Commercial or contractual project management responsibilities. **Key Interfaces** * BIM Project Manager (reporting and guidance) * Engineering Project Managers and Design Engineers * External consultants and coordination teams ****Why Join Us?**** At Ethos, we’re dedicated to fostering an environment that encourages both **technical excellence and personal well\-being** . **We offer:** * Flexible Fridays and a 9\-day fortnight working schedule — enjoy every second Friday off to maintain work–life balance. * A culture that values flexibility, learning, and teamwork, with clear progression pathways and support toward professional accreditation. * Opportunities to work alongside some of the most experienced engineers in the industry, within a forward\-thinking, technology\-driven consultancy. **Benefits:** * Flexible working times / Hybrid Working * 20 days of holidays, increasing up to 25 days * Pension Scheme * Your own company laptop * Coverage for working travel out\-of\-pocket expenses * Travel tax scheme or bike\-to\-work scheme * Regular training, mentoring, and further college support * Support for Chartership * A professional subscription fee paid by the company * Heavily subsidized social committee activities (5\-a\-side football, golf society, climbing club, and regular social events) Here at Ethos we believe in equal opportunities and that each individual can bring something ‘WoW’ to our organisation. So if you don't necessarily meet every single point on the job description, but think you have what it takes, please still get in touch. We'd love to have a chat and see if you could be a great fit. **At Ethos, our *vision* is to:** ***“Be the company people want to work with, and for”*** **Values** Our values differentiate and focus us every day to be **EPIC** \- for our people and our customers. Recruitment Agencies: Please do not reply to this job advert. Should Ethos require assistance with this posting you will be contacted by our Talent Acquisition Team.

title: Backend / Platform Engineer
description: **Backend / Platform Engineer** Induct builds low\-cost cloud connected access control systems for physical buildings. We build linux\-based controllers deployed in real buildings, with real, unreliable network conditions, designed to retrofit existing legacy systems, alongside a cloud platform enabling real\-time networking, device coordination, authentication, and fleet management. We're looking for a backend/systems engineer who enjoys owning difficult real\-world production systems. **What you'll work on:** \* Realtime networking infrastructure \* Device ↔ cloud communication systems \* Distributed coordination and reliability \* Device identity, authentication and security \* Event\-driven backend infrastructure \* Observability, debugging and production tooling \* Backend APIs and platform systems \* Device update orchestration and rollout systems \* Large\-scale Linux device fleet management \* Guide the architecture of the platform as it grows **What we care about** \* Strong engineering judgement \* Ownership and autonomy \* Interest in systems and infrastructure **Experience with the following is useful, but not required:** \* TypeScript (Node.js / NestJS) \* PostgreSQL \* Rust \* Distributed / event\-driven systems \* Kubernetes and cloud infrastructure \* Networking and reliability engineering \* Linux systems (server or embedded) **Role Details** This is an in\-person role in Dublin City. We're a lightweight fast moving engineering team, so there's a high degree of ownership and responsibility expected. You'll have significant influence on the architecture, infrastructure, and overall technical direction of the platform.

title: Business Analyst
description: Job Title: Business Analyst – Payments, Fraud \& SCA Location: Dublin (Onsite 1 day per week) Contract Duration: Initial 6\-month contract (extendable) Start Date: ASAP Role Overview We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with strong expertise in Payments, Fraud, and Strong Customer Authentication (SCA) to join the Banking Consulting Group. The successful candidate will play a key role across the full project lifecycle, including requirements gathering, process mapping, solution architecture and design, development support, UAT, and deployment. The ideal candidate will have 6–10 years of experience within the banking and financial services sector, with a strong background in large\-scale regulatory and payments transformation projects. This role requires close collaboration with client business and IT stakeholders, as well as internal development teams, to deliver high\-quality payment solutions. Key Responsibilities Engage with stakeholders to gather and analyse business requirements Map and document business processes and payment workflows Contribute to solution architecture and functional design discussions Support development, testing, UAT, and deployment activities Act as a Subject Matter Expert (SME) in Payments, Fraud, and SCA Lead domain discussions and provide consultancy to BFSI clients Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure successful project delivery Required Skills \& Experience 8\+ years of experience in Payments domain Strong expertise in Fraud Prevention and SCA regulations/processes Proven experience in Business Analysis for Payments solutions Experience working on large regulatory or transformation programmes Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills Ability to lead functional and domain discussions with BFSI customers Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and documentation skills

title: Business Development Representative
description: *If you are a current employee who is interested in applying to this position, please navigate to the internal* *Careers* *site to apply.* **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. We are excited to have you apply to join our MarketStar team and can’t wait to discuss how we can help you find growth! **About the Business Development Representative** MarketStar is looking for a sales professional to represent our client as they continue their successful expansion into the enterprise corporate space. This is an exceptional opportunity to be a strategic leader on a pivotal team at the forefront of the latest technology and work with one of the top three companies in the world. Your responsibility will be to generate and qualify sales‑qualified opportunities within complex enterprise and corporate accounts. You will be a key contributor to MarketStar's mission by developing and executing strategic outreach plans and acting as a subject matter expert on how our client’s solutions can transform business operations. You will cover the EMEA Enterprise and Corporate markets, but may also provide mentorship and strategic support across other regions. **Location:** Hybrid, Leopardstown , Dublin 18 (Tuesday, Wednesday \& Thursday in office) **What Will You Do?** * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Contact potential clients through cold calls and emails with the power of the brand behind you * Explore new business opportunities within certain target industries and personas * Present product value to potential clients * Identify client needs and suggest appropriate solutions * Proactively seek new business opportunities in the market * Set up meetings or calls between (prospective) clients and enterprise sales team * Adhere to daily, weekly and monthly KPIs * Achieve established sales goals and objectives * Serve as product and service advocate for the client, build interest and awareness of client’s value proposition, and assess mutual fit * Learn and maintain a current, comprehensive knowledge of client’s products and services * Champion our success stories and share with targeted companies in interesting industries and territories. **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * Fluent in English (Read, Write \& Speak) * 2 years of BDR experience * Experience working with IT decision makers * Experience with outreach sales strategies and prospecting companies in the EMEA region * Highly driven with a passion for sales and for developing as a sales professional * Excellent written and verbal communication skills * Track record of achieving sales quotas * Business Development or sales experience (SaaS experience preferable) * Strong sales aptitude skills, solutions oriented with a passion for the customer and the customer experience * CRM, Salesforce, SalesLoft, Outreach.IO and other sales tech stack experience preferred. * Tech savvy with a passion for technology and the ability to learn, comprehend , and explain technology. * Experience self\-prospecting leads and building your own pipeline **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Employer\-Matched Pension \& Education reimbursement (After 6 Months) * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is base salary per annum of €* *40,000* *with an* *additional* *variable bonus* *component* *of €* *20,000\.* *There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process* *.* *MarketStar is* *firmly committed* *to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable* *accommodations are* *available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual who identifies as someone with a disability and require reasonable accommodation(s) to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at* people.success@marketstar.com *for* *assistance* *.*

title: Business Support, Administrative Assistant
description: **JLL** Our people at JLL and JLL Technologies are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology for our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people and empowering them to thrive, grow meaningful careers and to find a place where they belong. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades or technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, join our team as we help shape a brighter way forward This is a part\-time role, offering flexibility and freedom in structuring working hours, whether through full days or half days, accommodating both business needs and personal work\-life balance preferences. **General** * Provide full administrative support to the team in the office. * Coordinate with the offshore PDS Business Support team in the JLL Business Services Function (JBS) for designated tasks and business processes.. Example tasks include but not limited to: * Booking travel and processing expenses Scheduling meetings, booking meeting rooms and coordinating refreshments as required. * Typing and formatting of technical reports and presentations. Utilising other remote options such as backup typing as necessary. * Document production using Microsoft Office 365 software including Nitro/Adobe. * Co\-ordination of new starters/leavers process in conjunction with PDS Operations team and local office protocols. * Set up project files and maintain accurate filing according in iManage * Maintain accurate equipment records and inventory management * Maintain client contact details in CRM system i.e. Salesforce * Deal with staff and client queries as required. **Communications** * Coordinate and distribute team communications, announcements, and policy updates * Actively facilitate communication flow between local office, national business support and operational teams * Manage team distribution lists and ensure accurate contact information * Maintain professional telephone and email etiquette as first point of contact * Organise and coordinate internal team meetings * Prepare meeting materials, agendas, and presentation packages in accordance with best practice QA and branding standards * Prepare and distribute meeting minutes and action item summaries as required using JLL GPT. * Serve as a communication bridge between technical teams, management, and support functions * Coordinate with other JLL departments (HR, IT, Finance) for team requirements. * Coordinate with the PDS Pursuits Team for timely marketing title: CLO Deal Administrator - 12 Month Fixed Term Contract
description: At U.S. Bank, we’re on a journey to do our best. Helping the customers and businesses we serve to make better and smarter financial decisions, enabling the communities we support to grow and succeed in the right ways, all more confidently and more often—that’s what we call the courage to thrive. We believe it takes all of us to bring our shared ambition to life, and each person is unique in their potential. A career with U.S. Bank gives you a wide, ever\-growing range of opportunities to discover what makes you thrive. Try new things, learn new skills and discover what you excel at—all from Day One. As a wholly owned subsidiary of U.S. Bank, Elavon is committed to building the platforms and ecosystems that help over 1\.5 million customers around the world to achieve their financial goals—no matter what they need. From transaction processing to customer service, to driving innovation and launching new products, we’re building a range of tailored payment solutions powered by the latest technology. As part of our team, you can explore what motivates and energizes your career goals: partnering with our customers, our communities, and each other. We actively uphold transparent and fair hiring practices that support individual opportunity, inclusive culture, and career mobility across all levels of our organisation. We offer meaningful opportunities for growth, a culture of inclusion, and a strong commitment to transparency and integrity in everything we do. **Job Description** **CLO Deal Administrator \- 12 Month Fixed Term Contract** The CLO division sits within US Bank’s Global Corporate Trust (GCT) division. CLO’s are structured finance vehicles that issue both debt and equity liabilities, with the proceeds being used to buy a portfolio of collateral. Typical types of collateral include syndicated bank loans, corporate bonds \& equity. The CLO Deal Administrator role is a key role within our Corporate Trust Business and offers a good level of individual responsibility and accountability, whilst being part of a dynamic and successful business line with opportunity for future growth and development. Responsibilities of this role include: oversight and execution of bond/bank loan trade settlements, analysis of Money Market Fund investment requirements, booking cash inflows/outflows to the portfolio, tracking asset attributes such as accrual information, providing cash \& asset reporting to the clients on a daily basis, and assisting the Analysts with reporting to the CLO investors. The Deal Administrator ensures 100% accuracy and timeliness of data through the booking and reconciliation of account activity. Context for this role The role holder will be required to build and maintain effective relationships across the Bank and its parent, as well as with external parties, clients, and stakeholders. The role holder will be part of a specialist team and will be expected to exhibit teamwork, attention to detail, ability to work to tight deadlines, detailed technical knowledge with the ability to manage a heavy and varied workload. **Key Responsibilities** * Cash \& Asset Reconciliation responsibility for a portfolio of CDO/CLO transactions * Generate accurate Daily Cash Reports that reconcile asset payments and receivables on an Actual vs Expected model basis * Investigate and resolve any exceptions / discrepancies arising. Ability to resolve transactional and portfolio related issues * Process Broadly Syndicated Loan trades from trade ticket to settlement/payment request stage * Analysis and oversight of automated Trade settlement processes * Daily Review of available cash balances \& Money Market Funds to ensure sufficient liquidity to action trade settlements * Receive Bond/Securities tickets and Loan Trade Documents from Clients, review for accuracy, complete relevant documentation and liaise with internal Operations Team’s to ensure they settle within agreed time scales in the market * Liaise directly with Clients/Counterparties/Agent Banks and other departments for Bond/Loan \& Cash related queries * Settle Loan and Bond transactions on internal compliance system – CDO Suite * Reconcile Cash \& Asset Balances with our clients to support CDO Analytics in the production of Monthly CDO Investor Report **Experience Required** * Typically 1\-2 years experience within Financial Services with experience of finance / cash recs / settlements /or in a relatable Operations Analyst role. * Open to graduates with some internship / entry level experience related to the role / skills needed. * Preferably with any knowledge of fixed income, debt, loans or derivatives and a passion to enter the CLO market or some previous CLO experience or theoretical knowledge of CLO's be hugely beneficial * Looking for someone with a good level of numeracy and attention to detail * Excellent verbal and written communication skills and a client centric approach * Ability to work both independently and as a team player * Strong organizational skills and detail\-oriented * Relationship building across several internal groups \& divisions * Ability to manage a heavy and varied workload while demonstrating strong attention to detail * Indenture/Credit Agreement interpretation abilities and analytical skills are preferrable **Location expectations** This role requires working from a U.S. Bank location three (3\) or more days per week. This role is aligned to our Cherrywood Office **Accessibility** We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need adjustments at any stage of the application or hiring process, please contact your recruiter for guidance and support. **Total Rewards** U.S. Bank is committed to fair, equitable, and transparent compensation practices in line with local regulatory and legal requirements. Our total rewards approach is designed to attract, retain, and support top talent while ensuring equal pay for work of equal value. We offer a market\-competitive compensation package that includes: * Clearly defined salary ranges aligned with industry benchmarks and internal equity standards. * Performance\-based incentives for eligible employees (as defined by relevant plan rules), awarded through transparent, objective criteria that recognize both individual and company performance. * Inclusive equitable benefits that are accessible to all employees and focused around our 3 main pillars of financial wellbeing, health \& wellness). * Continuous development opportunities including training, education support, and career progression pathways based on inclusive and transparent criteria. * Employee recognition programs that celebrate achievements and milestones for all. We regularly review our compensation and benefits to ensure they remain competitive, inclusive, and responsive to employee needs and market trends. Further details of the compensation package will be provided upon application. We encourage candidates to explore the full value of our offer, including monetary and non\-monetary benefits, at Employee benefits and development \| U.S. Bank \| Elavon. **Closing Date** Posting may be closed earlier due to high volume of applicants. We aim to provide timely updates throughout the process and encourage early applications to ensure consideration.

title: Case Official – Credit Collections & Support Team – 6 Month Fixed Term Contract
description: At IVC Evidensia, we are a leading veterinary services provider in Europe and North America with more than 2,500 clinics and hospitals across 19 countries and around 42,000 employees. We aim to have an extraordinary impact on animal lives, supporting in the region of 25,000 animals every single day across our international network. IVC Evidensia exists to champion the long\-term health and welfare of animals and deliver our purpose of Healthy Animals, Happy Owners. At IVC Evidensia, our Central Collections team plays a vital role in supporting our veterinary practices and clients by managing outstanding balances in a fair, professional, and customer\-focused way. As a Case Official, you will be responsible for managing client accounts, engaging with clients through inbound and outbound contact, agreeing appropriate repayment solutions, and resolving cases efficiently while maintaining high standards of service, compliance, and empathy. This is an entry\-level role and a great opportunity for someone looking to build experience in a structured, supportive collections or customer contact environment, with clear development pathways into senior and leadership roles. **This role is being offered on a 6\-month fixed term contract basis.** **Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities** **Client \& Case Management** * Manage client accounts through inbound and outbound communication channels (phone, email, letters). * Engage with clients to understand their circumstances and agree appropriate repayment solutions. * Resolve assigned cases through standard processes, ensuring timely and accurate outcomes. * Maintain a professional, empathetic, and customer\-focused approach in all interactions. **Operational Delivery** * Work to agreed treatment strategies, policies, and processes to deliver consistent outcomes. * Meet individual productivity, quality, and compliance targets. * Maintain accurate and complete case notes, records, and documentation. * Escalate complex, sensitive, or vulnerable cases appropriately to Senior Case Officials or Team Leaders. **Compliance, Risk \& Quality** * Adhere to Debt Management Policy, Client Vulnerability Policy, Data\-Protection Policy and standards, and complaints procedures as well as our IVC Code of Responsibility. * Identify and flag potential vulnerability indicators and follow agreed support processes. * Participate in quality assurance activities and respond positively to feedback and coaching. * Maintain auditable records and follow required controls and procedures. **Team \& Continuous Improvement** * Participate in training, coaching, and team development activities. * Contribute to a positive team culture focused on accountability, learning, and continuous improvement. * Share feedback and ideas to improve client experience and operational effectiveness. **Experience/Qualifications** **Essential:** * Experience in a customer service, contact\-centre, collections, or client\-facing role. * Confident communicator with the ability to handle sensitive or challenging conversations calmly and professionally. * Strong attention to detail with the ability to follow processes and maintain accurate records. * Basic numeracy and literacy skills, with confidence using systems and technology. * Willingness to learn, develop, and take on feedback. **Desirable** * Previous experience in collections * Experience working in a regulated or client\-sensitive environment (e.g. healthcare, financial services, utilities). * Awareness of vulnerability or affordability considerations (training will be provided). Following receipt of your application, you will be contacted by one of our experienced hire recruitment team. **As a BAME and LGBTQ\+ inclusive employer, we are keen to hear from candidates from all minority and diverse groups. As a Disability Confident Employer, we are keen to hear from candidates with disabilities and long\-term health conditions and would be happy to discuss any reasonable adjustments needed during the recruitment process.** We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Chief Operating Officer
description: **About the Company and Position** Strata Financial is a fast\-growing full\-service accountancy firm strategically set to become one of Ireland’s Top 10 accounting firms by 2028, located across 6 offices in the North East/South East Corridor of the island of Ireland. As the group scales through both organic growth, and integration of other firms, we are now seeking to recruit a hands\-on Chief Operating Officer to build the operational engine that allows every office, team and newly acquired firm to perform consistently. This is a role for a practical operator — someone who can standardise, integrate, improve and execute. You will own the day\-to\-day operational agenda, create the systems and controls that support profitable growth, and turn each joining firm into a smoothly integrated part of the wider group. **Key Responsibilities** * Build and embed a consistent group\-wide operating model across compliance, audit, accounting, tax, advisory, bookkeeping, payroll and client onboarding. * Create clear workflows, file structures, review points, SLAs, quality controls, KPIs, dashboards and management rhythms across every office and service line. * Improve utilisation, recovery, WIP, lock\-up, write\-offs, client throughput and overall operational performance through stronger accountability and better data. * Remove duplication, manual processes and operational drag across legacy and acquired offices, replacing them with smarter processes, shared services and technology. * Own and rationalise the practice technology stack across practice management, document management, workflow, time and billing, payroll, ID and AML, and client portals. * Lead post\-acquisition integration through a repeatable 30/60/90/180\-day playbook covering systems, people, clients, processes, premises and brand. * Manage systems migrations end\-to\-end, including scoping, suppliers, data migration, parallel running, cutover, adoption and legacy system retirement, with no service disruption. * Work with HR, IT, Finance, Marketing, technical and risk teams to align contracts, roles, benefits, performance frameworks, ways of working, cultural issues and regulatory standards across ICAEW, ACCA, AAT and wider requirements. * Track integration milestones, cost\-to\-serve gains, system savings and synergies, reporting clearly to the CEO, CFO and board. * Support M\&A diligence by assessing systems debt, process maturity, staffing dependencies, client concentration, operational risk and realistic integration cost. * Chair integration governance and advise the leadership team on capacity, pricing, service\-line growth and capital deployment. **Experience/Requirements** * Proven experience as a COO, Operations Director, Head of Integration, Head of Transformation or similar within accountancy, professional services, legal, consulting, wealth management or another fee\-earning environment. * Direct buy\-and\-build or roll\-up experience, ideally PE\-backed, with hands\-on involvement in at least three completed integrations. * Strong knowledge of accountancy practice operations, including compliance workflow, advisory delivery, WIP, lock\-up, recovery, utilisation and partner economics. * Strong fluency with systems such as Drive, CCH, IRIS, Karbon, Xero, Sage, Dext, BrightPay, Senta, Inflo, FYI, Virtual Cabinet or equivalent platforms. * A track record of delivering systems migrations, operational KPIs, dashboards and governance in a partnership or partner\-led environment. * Relevant third level qualification would be useful, but credibility with partners and acquired\-firm principals matters more. * Commercially sharp, execution\-first, data\-led, politically astute, resilient, pragmatic and highly organised under pressure. This is not a strategy\-only seat, a deputy CEO role or a polished operating environment. Strata Financial is mid\-build, with working, but developing systems, developing processes and a lot more deals ahead. The mission is simple: fix it, scale it, and make every future integration easier and better than the last, and deliver effective excellence. **Remuneration Package** Highly Competitive Salary and Benefits. **Applications** Applications should include a CV and a short covering note setting out: (i) two integrations the candidate has personally led, (ii) the operational KPI they are most proud of having moved, and (iii) their view on the most common reason accountancy firm integrations underperform their deal model. **Contact** To apply, please submit your application or make an initial enquiry please contact: Yvonne McNulty, Senior Partner, at Brightwater Executive to stratafinancial@brightwaterexecutive.com

title: Civil Engineer
description: **Civil Engineer** ================== **Role Overview** We are seeking a Civil Engineer with 5\+ years’ experience to join our growing civil engineering team based in Dublin City Centre. The successful candidate will lead the design and delivery of site infrastructure to support development, including water, drainage and road design. The ideal applicant for this role will be eager to develop their skills and progress to a Senior role within the organisation. **About Us** CS Consulting is a leading civil, structural traffic engineering consultancy delivering practical, buildable solutions across Ireland and internationally. We work on technically diverse building and infrastructure projects, from concept through to delivery, with a strong focus on constructability, quality, and collaboration. We’re a hands\-on practice where engineers are trusted to lead, influence design, and shape project outcomes. We offer a supportive, solutions\-driven environment, meaningful project exposure, and the opportunity to play a key role in the continued growth and direction of the business. **Upcoming Projects** Our upcoming projects include all site development and infrastructure work (roads, traffic transport, drainage, water, utilities, bridges, etc.) from feasibility through planning, detailed design and construction for projects including: * Large\-scale residential projects for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and their Development Partners * Private residential projects (high\-rise and low\-rise) nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and public/ private sector Clients * New\-build commercial office and hotel projects * Commercial office and hotel refurbishment and fit\-out projects * Industrial and data centre projects **Key Responsibilities** * Design of SuDs features * Design of Foul and Watermain Systems to Uisce Eireann Code of Practice and Local Authority requirements * Design of roads to comply with TII legislation DMURS * Prepare feasibility and planning stage reports including engineering services reports, flood risk assessments, etc. Develop responses to planning conditions to secure compliance. * Prepare tender and construction level designs and specifications. * Attend design team/ client meetings, participate in stakeholder engagement, and liaise with Local Authorities/ Statutory Bodies on behalf of clients. * Collaborate with civil and structural teams on integrated multidisciplinary projects. **Qualifications Experience** * Honours Degree (Level 8\+) in Civil Engineering. * Chartered Engineer (CEng MIEI) or ambition to achieve chartership in the near future. * Minimum 5 years’ relevant experience, with at least 2 years in an Irish consultancy. * Strong knowledge of water, drainage and road design standards from planning through construction. * Proficient in AutoCAD, road design software (Civil 3D desirable) and drainage modelling software (InfoDrainage or similar). * Full driving licence **Key Attributes** * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Capable of developing strong client relationships and representing the company at design team, local authority and public meetings. * Detail\-oriented with a strong technical foundation and practical site awareness. * Ambitious and proactive, with a commitment to CPD and technical leadership. * Comfortable working both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team. **Why Join Us?** * Competitive Salary and Opportunities for Progression: We offer a competitive salary and bonus, as well as a clear pathway to Senior * Mid\-Size Firm with Large\-Scale Projects: Opportunity to take ownership of your work with the support of highly experienced Senior Engineers and Directors as well as a talented team of engineering and technician resources. * Broad Range of Projects with the Best Clients: We work across commercial, residential, healthcare, education, hotel, leisure and retail with Ireland's top public and private Clients including the Land Development Agency (LDA), Ronan Group Real Estate, McGarrell Reilly, Quintain, Westridge RE, DRES, Hibernia Real Estate Group, Bartra, Tetrarch, Local Authorities, University of Limerick, Ireland's Top Contractors, and many more. * Shape the way we work: Our Organisation builds on energetic colleagues with a desire to learn, contribute their experience and improve our way of working. We believe in innovation and improvement and utilise data analysis to learn and grow. * CPD Learning: Engineers Ireland approved CPD programme and regular lessons learned and knowledge sharing sessions in a round\-table format. * Leading Software Tech: Access to leading technical software and review our requirements regularly to ensure we are using the best and latest in technology.

title: Civil Site Supervisor
description: **Are you looking to become part of a collaborative team whilst maximising your potential to grow and develop within your career? Apply today.** **The Role.** The **Civil Site Supervisor** will play a key role in managing the daily operations of multiple civil crews working across various sites. This position requires excellent coordination, communication, and organisational skills to ensure all works are carried out safely, efficiently, and to the highest quality standards. **Key Duties and Responsibilities will include but are not limited to:** * Responsible for running numerous projects within urban and rural environments. * Liaising and corresponding with Clients and Client Representatives to enable the successful delivery of projects and building of relationships. * Reviewing drawings with a high level of detail. * Amending designs in line with feedback received. * delivery processes. * Mentor designers and identify training needs for junior site team. * Monitor progress of site work, ensuring work is completed on time and within budget. * Planning and overseeing the delivery of materials and manage any discrepancies where appropriate. * Ensuring that all work complies with client requirements and safety legislation alongside other legal requirements. * Responsible for overseeing the traffic management as well as health and safety on\-site and ensuring all safety checks are carried out. * Liaise with Local Authorities with the inspection of footway conditions prior and post installation works. * Liaise with the workflow department regarding the programming and scheduling of work. * Provide Clients with a status report and act as first point of contact for members of the public and sub\-contractors. * Professionally handle complaints received from customers regarding carried out work. * Collaborate with clients to enable them to maximise the value of their infrastructure. * Quality control of works. * Manage required administrative tasks efficiently and effectively. * Ad hoc tasks as required. **The Candidate: Required Experience and Competencies:** * Full clean driver's license essential. * A qualification in engineering or equivalent is desirable. * Civils, drainage, or utilities (water \& electricity) experience. * Previous experience and the ability to read designs. * Commercially aware of projects costs. * Experience with site activities and multiple teams. * Good knowledge of HSQE requirements. * Good knowledge of ground support systems. * Computer literate. * Confident, motivated and can work independently as well as part of a team. * Ability to work under pressure with strict deadlines. * Time management abilities to meet targets. * Good communication, interpersonal, and customer service skills. * Integrity and respect for confidentiality and privacy. **Become a part of something bigger.** At the heart of GMC is our people, from engineers through to customer service and we aim to work collaboratively towards our common goals. In GMC we foster a work culture that promotes collaboration and learning, building an environment where our people have the capacity to maximise their potential whilst gaining the opportunity to continuously develop. **Our Future.** Since the founding of GMC in 1981 by Gerry McCloskey, GMC has grown to be Ireland's leading Civils and Utilities company and the future for GMC is bright. We have engaged with our staff and other stakeholders to agree and publish our core values. One of our core values is to *"Focus on the Future"* . Our growth to date reflects the confidence that our customers have in our service delivery, in the innovative approach that we bring to how we do our work, and to our culture of safety and quality. We have ambitious plans to develop our footprint in Ireland and overseas but understand that every decision we make contributes to people's quality of life throughout generations, and we don't take that responsibility lightly as we build Ireland's infrastructure of tomorrow, today.
