title: Marketing Intern
description: **DIGITAL MARKETING** Digital Marketing Internship at Babylon Radio Company: Babylon Radio Location: Dublin, Ireland (Remote work available) Type: Unpaid Internship Duration: Flexible (Part\-time or Full\-time) **About Us:** Babylon Radio is a multicultural platform dedicated to promoting integration in Ireland. We provide music, news, and cultural content to a wide audience through our online radio station and website. Join our passionate team and contribute to a dynamic media environment. **Role Overview:** Are you enthusiastic about digital marketing and eager to gain practical experience? We are looking for a Digital Marketing Intern to support our marketing efforts and help expand our online presence. This is an excellent opportunity to enhance your skills and build your portfolio. **Key Responsibilities:** * Develop and implement social media strategies across various platforms (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, etc.) * Create engaging content, including posts, images, and short videos * Assist in managing and updating our website and blog * Monitor and analyze web traffic and user engagement metrics * Research market trends and competitor activities * Collaborate with the team to plan and execute marketing campaigns **What We're Looking For:** * Strong interest in digital marketing and social media * Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English * Creativity and a keen eye for detail * Familiarity with social media analytics tools is a plus * Ability to work independently and within a team * Previous experience in marketing or related fields is advantageous but not essential **What We Offer:** * Hands\-on experience in digital marketing within a multicultural environment * Opportunity to contribute ideas and see them come to life * Flexible working hours with the possibility of remote work * Mentorship and guidance from experienced professionals * A reference letter upon successful completion of the internship **How to Apply:** If you're excited to make a real impact and grow with us, send your CV and a brief cover letter to info@babylonradio.com with the subject line "Digital Marketing Internship Application."

title: Maximo Junior Consultant
description: At Nucleo, we are a purpose\-driven advisory and technology consulting firm specialising in Enterprise Solutions and Data \& AI. We partner with ambitious organisations to deliver transformative projects that create measurable, long\-lasting value. Collaboration is at the core of everything we do—both with our clients and within our teams. With over 60 in\-house experts, we deliver bespoke, end\-to\-end solutions across ERP, CRM, asset management systems, automation, and advanced analytics. The result: seamless implementation, clear stakeholder alignment, and lasting business impact. **Our Core Offerings Include** * Modern Data Platforms (Microsoft Azure, Fabric, Databricks, Snowflake) * Data Engineering \& Analytics Solutions * AI \& Machine Learning Enablement * Business Intelligence \& Reporting (Power BI) * Data Governance \& Architecture * Enterprise Cloud \& Digital Transformation Projects The **Junior Consultant** role is designed for a technical professional who wants to develop a strong functional backbone. You will support the implementation of Maximo solutions, ensuring that technical builds align perfectly with the client’s operational workflows. **Key Responsibilities** * Technical Execution: Build and deploy configurations using Application Designer, Data Configuration, and basic Automation Scripts. * Functional Analysis: Support Senior Consultants in documenting "As\-Is" and "To\-Be" business processes. * Integration \& Data: Facilitate data mapping and loading (MIF/SQL) and develop custom reports based on functional requirements. * Testing \& Quality: Lead User Acceptance Testing (UAT) sessions, ensuring the technical solution meets the functional "success criteria" defined by the client. * Training: Assist in developing training manuals and conducting end\-user training sessions. **Technical \& Functional Requirements** * Experience: 3–5 years of Maximo experience with a focus on configuration. * Functional: Understanding of EAM (Enterprise Asset Management) concepts like work management, PR/PO, PM scheduling, failure codes, and inventory valuation. * Technical: Strong SQL skills, familiarity with BIRT, and experience with Maximo’s internal configuration tools like conditions, DB, domains, escalations etc. * Consulting: Strong presentation skills and the ability to document processes clearly. **General Skills** * Problem\-solving and analytical skills * Understands SDLC * Maximo Certifications * Cloud Certifications (optional) **This role offers hybrid working**

title: Mechanical Engineer- Dublin
description: Mechanical Engineer Don’t join an Engineering firm. Build what comes next. Most roles ask you to deliver drawings. This one expects you to shape systems that change how buildings perform and how people live. The Role You’ll work across the full project lifecycle, designing mechanical systems and influencing decisions from day one. You won’t just deliver designs, you’ll help shape how projects perform, working closely with teams, clients, and stakeholders. What You'll Do * Design mechanical building services systems across multiple sectors * Deliver projects from concept through to construction and handover * Produce calculations, specifications, and technical reports * Coordinate with electrical engineers, architects, and design teams * Attend client meetings and contribute to engineering decisions * Support site inspections, commissioning, and final delivery * Drive energy\-efficient and sustainable design solutions What You'll Bring * Degree in Mechanical or Building Services Engineering * 2 to 5\+ years' experience in a consultancy or similar environment * Strong HVAC and building services design experience * Experience with IES, Hevacomp, or similar; Revit beneficial * Ability to manage workload across multiple projects * Strong communication and client\-facing skills What You'll Get This isn't built around perks. It's built around how high\-performing engineers actually want to work. Real ownership, real progression, and a culture that trusts you to deliver. *The Essentials* * Competitive salary, bonus and pension that reflect your impact * 22 to 27 days annual leave, plus bank holidays * Flexible working across a 37\.5 hour week: early start or later start? You decide *Career Progression* * A clear path forward, with responsibility increasing as you prove it * Direct exposure to senior leaders, not layers of hierarchy * IN2 Academy, CPD support and chartership guidance * Fully paid professional fees *Health and Wellbeing* * Employee Assistance Programme and annual health checks * Wellbeing and fitness initiatives that support you long term * Cycle to Work scheme to make the everyday easier *Social and Community* * Genuinely strong team culture, built through regular events and shared wins * Employee\-led charity initiatives with real involvement * Offices in Dublin, London, Berlin, Belfast, Athlone and Madrid Why IN2? Across every role, electrical, mechanical, or BIM, the IN2 difference is the same: you're expected to think, lead, and shape outcomes, not just execute instructions. Here's what sets us apart. Ownership from Day One Whether you're an Engineer or a Senior, you'll have real accountability and decision\-making authority. No waiting for permission. No layers of sign\-off slowing you down. Progression Based on Ability Faster progression based on what you deliver, not how long you've been in the seat. A clear pathway from Engineer to Senior, Associate, and beyond. Innovation Time Dedicated time to develop new ideas, explore emerging technologies, and contribute to sustainability strategies: built into how we work, not bolted on. High\-Performance, Low\-Ego Culture A team that challenges each other, supports each other, and wins together. No hierarchy for hierarchy's sake. Just talented people doing exceptional work. Complex, High\-Profile Projects Exposure to design\-led, high\-impact developments that push the boundaries of what building services engineering can achieve. Global Reach Work across offices in Dublin, London, Berlin, Belfast, Athlone, and Madrid\- a genuinely international practice with a collaborative, connected culture. *Ready to own the outcome? Join a team that expects more and gives more in return.*

title: Meetings & Events Executive
description: **Company Description** The 4\-star Glenview Hotel \& Leisure Club, located in North Wicklow just off the N11, provides a unique venue for corporate meetings and events. With eight modern and well\-lit conference rooms accommodating up to 180 delegates, the hotel is equipped with state\-of\-the\-art AV technology, making it ideal for training sessions, board meetings, or product launches. Guests can enjoy elegant accommodations with luxurious amenities, including access to the Glenview Leisure Club. The landscaped gardens offer a picturesque setting for events, with options for outdoor gatherings like BBQs and smaller private parties. Located just 10 minutes from Cherrywood and offering ample parking, the Glenview Hotel is an exceptional destination for both business and leisure. **Role Description** This is a full\-time on\-site role for a Meetings \& Events Executive, based in County Wicklow, Ireland. The role involves coordinating and managing all aspects of corporate and social events hosted at the hotel. Responsibilities include handling client inquiries, planning and executing event logistics, managing budgets, liaising with other departments to ensure smooth operations, and maintaining communication with clients to deliver exceptional service. As the primary point of contact for events, the Meetings \& Events Executive will work to uphold the hotel’s reputation for excellence while creating memorable experiences for guests. **Qualifications** * Event Coordination and Planning skills * Venue and Vendor Management expertise * Experience with budget management and logistical planning * Strong customer service and interpersonal skills * Ability to work collaboratively with diverse teams * Excellent organizational and time\-management skills * Proficiency in office software and event management tools * Flexibility to work in a dynamic, on\-site environment * Previous experience in hospitality or event management preferred * Relevant formal education or certifications in event management, hospitality, or a related field

title: Microsoft Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Microsoft Endpoint \& Identity Engineer to support modern workplace and endpoint management initiatives within a large enterprise environment. This contract role will focus on Microsoft Intune, Microsoft 365, endpoint management, identity services, and modern deployment technologies including Windows Autopilot and Windows Hello for Business. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and optimisation of Microsoft Intune / Endpoint Manager * Design and support of endpoint enrolment and device lifecycle management processes * Deployment and support of: * Windows Autopilot * Windows Hello for Business * Management of device compliance policies, configuration profiles, and application deployments * Integration and administration of Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) services * Support of identity and access management controls including Conditional Access and device\-based access policies * Troubleshooting endpoint management, enrolment, and policy\-related issues * Support modern workplace transformation and security improvement initiatives * Production of technical documentation and operational procedures * Collaboration with internal IT teams and knowledge transfer activities **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5\+ years’ experience in Microsoft infrastructure, endpoint management, or modern workplace engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience with: * Microsoft Intune / Endpoint Manager * Microsoft 365 administration * Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) * Experience managing endpoint compliance, device policies, and application deployments * Strong troubleshooting capability across endpoint and identity technologies * Experience supporting enterprise Windows environments **Desirable Experience** * End\-to\-end Autopilot deployment experience * Experience implementing Windows Hello for Business * Knowledge of identity governance and access management principles * Experience delivering endpoint modernisation or transformation projects * Familiarity with security\-focused device management practices **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Network Engineer
description: Our client, based in Dublin, is seeking an experienced Senior Network Engineer to support and enhance enterprise network infrastructure within a large\-scale operational environment. This is a hands\-on contract role focused on firewall administration, network security operations, and infrastructure support across on\-premise and cloud environments. The successful candidate will play a key role in maintaining secure, resilient, and high\-performing network services. **Key Responsibilities** * Administration and support of enterprise firewall platforms including technologies such as Fortinet, Check Point, Palo Alto, or Azure Firewall * Management of firewall policies, NAT, VPN connectivity (site\-to\-site and remote access), and security rulebases * Troubleshooting complex network, routing, and connectivity issues across LAN, WAN, and cloud environments * Support and maintenance of core network services including switching, routing, DNS, DHCP, and network segmentation * Implementation and support of network security controls in collaboration with cybersecurity teams * Participation in infrastructure upgrades, network changes, and implementation projects * Operational support for cloud networking environments, preferably Microsoft Azure * Maintenance of network documentation, configurations, and operational procedures * Knowledge transfer and collaboration with internal technical teams **Required Skills \& Experience** * 5–10\+ years’ experience in Network Engineering or Infrastructure Engineering roles * Strong hands\-on experience managing enterprise firewall environments * Excellent troubleshooting and diagnostic capability across complex networks * Experience supporting enterprise LAN/WAN infrastructure * Strong understanding of networking protocols and security principles * Ability to work independently in a fast\-paced operational environment **Desirable Experience** * Experience with Azure networking and cloud security services * Exposure to regulated or compliance\-driven environments * Familiarity with security frameworks and governance standards * Experience working within large enterprise IT operations teams **How to Apply** To apply, please send your latest CV outlining your relevant experience.

title: Night Audit
description: **Company Description** **Our heart beats turquoise.** Working at Motel One is like coming home to friends, where you can be your true self and where appreciation and support matter. We meet on an equal footing and see ourselves as one team: from our management to our trainees. We create a home away from home for our guests with our shared passion for modern design and high\-quality service. Our more than 80 hotels in 12 countries impress with exclusive design, high service standards and first\-class inner\-city locations at an attractive price. As the founder and pioneer of the budget design concept, we are successfully expanding in the metropolises of Europe and beyond. Become part of our team and develop Motel One with us. **Job Description** Looking after our guests in a professional manner at the front desk is extremely important at all times – from a warm welcome at check\-in until their departure, your job will be to assist our early risers and night owls. Your duties will also include the following: * Preparing daily closing balances * Cash transactions and cashing up * Processing booking requests * Preparing for breakfast **Qualifications** Whether you are a hotel professional or changing careers – you will fit in here! Because personality and hosting skills are our priority. You will also: * Have an excellent sense of quality and service, as well as hosting skills and a passion for the hotel industry * Be open, communicative and confident and have a solid command of English * Be a team player and willing to provide assistance across departments – we achieve our results together * Applicants must be able to speak, read, and understand English to effectively communicate in the role.

title: Night Team Member
description: Staycity is an urban aparthotel brand, blending the freedom of apartment living with the quality and consistency of a hotel stay. With locations in top European cities like Dublin, London, Paris, and Edinburgh, we provide home comforts in prime city spots — ideal for the modern traveller. Our mission is to help guests experience the city on their own terms — combining independence and hospitality in every stay. We’re a team of doers, thinkers, and hospitality lovers, committed to delivering standout guest experiences and building a culture we’re proud of. We’re now hiring a **Night Team Member** to help shape the Staycity guest journey. This role is will include duties across Reception and Food \& Beverage. **Benefits:** * Paid family leave (\>1 year of service) * Bonus scheme that rewards high performers;\- based on our core values and tied to individual property goals * Discounted rates for overnight stays for you, your family and friends * Refer and earn scheme \- earn up to £/€550 * Cycle to work scheme\- to support a healthy lifestyle and our planet * Volunteer days: 2 paid volunteer days per year * Support for you and your family when you need it with our Employee Assistance Program (EAP) * An extra day off to celebrate your birthday **We would love you to have:** * Previous experience working in a similar property is helpful but not a “must have” as full training will be provided * Prior experience using Opera is helpful but not a “must have” as full training will be provided * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills are required for this role. **What you can do for us:** * Be a Brand Ambassador: Embrace your role with joy, passion, and enthusiasm as you represent Staycity Group * Make a good first impression with all our guests: Confidence and great communication are vital in leaving lasting impressions. **Join us and be part of the journey.**

title: Occupational Therapist
description: ### **Description** **Join Our Team as a Full\-Time Occupational Therapist at The Marlay.** Are you a dedicated and passionate Occupational Therapist looking for a rewarding role across Nursing Home services? CareChoice is expanding our Allied Health team, and we are now inviting applications for full\-time Occupational Therapist position in The Marlay. **Why Choose CareChoice?** At CareChoice, we are committed to delivering exceptional, person\-centred care across our network of facilities. With over two decades of experience and a growing portfolio of specialist rehabilitation and residential services, we are continually developing innovative models of care to support residents and patients with diverse and complex needs. Join us as we expand our multidisciplinary teams and strengthen our clinical services across the CareChoice Group. **Responsibilities:** * Be responsible for managing own caseload and for assessment, planning, implementation and evaluation of treatment/ intervention programmes for service users according to service standards * Assessments to include but are not limited to seating assessments, complete functional assessments, complete hand assessments, cognitive assessments and recommendations on environmental layout. * Collaborate with service users, family, carers and other staff in treatment/intervention planning and in the provision of support and advice. * Plan discharge or transition of the service user between services as appropriate. * Ensure the quality of documentation of all assessments, treatment plans, progress notes, reports and discharge summaries are in accordance with professional standards * Foster and maintain professional working relationships with colleagues, front\-line managers, and other healthcare personnel in the team * Be involved in service planning and development by anticipating the changing needs of the service and service users for the inpatient service * Demonstrate a commitment to research and evidence\-based practice, identifying and contributing to research, clinical evaluation and audit opportunities * Document, and communicate verbally and / or in writing results of assessments, treatment/intervention programmes and recommendations to the team and relevant others in accordance with service policy * Arrange and carry out duties in a timely manner within settings appropriate to service users' needs and in line with local policy/guidelines. Attend review meetings, team meetings, case conferences, ward rounds etc. * Maintain quality standards of practice and participate in quality assurance and clinical audits as appropriate. * Be responsible for adhering to existing standards and protocols and for leading out on the development and maintenance of standards/strategies for quality improvement and outcome measurement * Incorporate national and international Evidence\-Based Occupational Therapy Practice (EBOTP) into their practice * Ensure that professional standards are maintained in relation to confidentiality, ethics and legislation * Operate within the scope of Occupational Therapy practice within Ireland and in accordance with local guidelines * Adhere to the Ethics for Occupational Therapists from CORU and the AOTI. **Eligibility criteria:** * Hold a recognised qualification BSc or MSc as an Occupational Therapist and have validation of your qualifications if you qualified outside the Republic of Ireland. * 2 year minimum, post\-graduate experience as an Occupational Therapist. * Registration with CORU. * Must have their own vehicle and full driving licence. * Excellent language and communication skills as accurate communication is essential. * Good interpersonal skills are essential. * All applicants must be legally entitled to work in Ireland.

title: Operational Technology (OT) Security Architect
description: The OT Security Architect is responsible for protecting industrial control systems (ICS) and operational technology environments against cyber threats while ensuring safety, availability, and regulatory compliance. The role bridges IT and OT, enabling secure digital operations across critical infrastructure environments such as utilities, energy, manufacturing, and transportation. Key Responsibilities OT Security Strategy \& Governance - Define and implement OT cybersecurity strategy, policies, and standards aligned to IEC 62443, NIST 800 82, NIS D, and ISO 27001\. - Establish OT security governance models and maintain OT risk registers across facilities. - Act as a trusted advisor to OT operations, engineering, and executive stakeholders. - Architecture \& Secure Design - Design secure OT network architectures aligned to the Purdue Model (Levels 0–3\.5\). - Define and validate secure IT/OT integration patterns, including data flows, remote access, and segmentation. - Review and secure digital transformation initiatives (e.g., historians, analytics platforms). - Threat \& Vulnerability Management - Conduct OT specific risk assessments, vulnerability assessments, and penetration testing of SCADA/DCS/PLC environments. - Implement vulnerability management and patching strategies suitable for safety critical systems. - Support OT incident response and forensic investigations. - Security Controls Implementation - Deploy and operate OT security technologies including: - OT Intrusion Detection / Network Monitoring (e.g., passive IDS) - Firewalls and segmentation controls at Levels 2 \& 3 - Secure remote access solutions - Removable media controls and data diodes - Support deployment across geographically dispersed and remote field sites. Compliance \& Regulatory Assurance - Support compliance with NIS D, sector specific regulations, and audit requirements. - Prepare evidence, security reports, and remediation plans for regulatory and internal audits. - Translate regulatory requirements into practical, site level security controls. - Stakeholder \& Delivery Management - Liaise with plant operators, OT engineers, OEMs, vendors, and system integrators. - Lead or support multi\-site OT security deployments and uplift programmes. - Provide training and awareness sessions for OT and SCADA teams. **Required Experience \& Skills** - Strong hands\-on experience in OT / ICS / SCADA / DCS environments. - Deep understanding of industrial networking, protocols, and architectures. - Proven experience delivering OT security assessments, designs, and implementations. - Knowledge of OT security standards and frameworks: - IEC 62443 - NIST 800 82 - NIS D - ISO 27001 (OT relevant controls) - Experience working in critical infrastructure or highly regulated environments.

title: Operations Administrator- 10 month contract
description: **Job Summary** Zurich Life Assurance plc is looking for an Operations Administrator within the Service Teams area of the business to focus on understanding and responding to what matters to customers. We are looking for a highly motivated individual with excellent communication skills. Successful candidates will gain a thorough knowledge of the workings of a life office by working in a dynamic environment handling queries and requests relating to products which range across Corporate and Individual Pensions, Investments \& Protection Plans. Initially, due to the nature of the role full time attendance in the office will be required but over time it will evolve into a hybrid based role. Please talk to us at interview about the flexibility you may need. This role may be available part\-time or full time. This is because we want the best people for our roles, and we recognise that sometimes those people aren’t available full\-time. Please talk to us at interview about the flexibility you may need. Your Role As an Operations Administrator your main responsibilities will include, but not necessarily be limited to, the following: * Ensuring that our customers come first is a key responsibility of the role * Processing all client/member requests through the new business stage / life cycle of their policies. This will involve ensuring that requests are processed in accordance with product rules and the company's processes and procedures * Dealing with clients/members and brokers/employers and other stakeholders over the phone * Complaint’s handling * Interacting with other areas of the business e.g. Sales, Propositions, Compliance etc. to improve how we work **Your Skills And Experience** As an Operations Administrator your skills and qualifications will include: * Good working knowledge of Microsoft Word, Excel * Third Level Qualification in relevant area * Have achieved or be making significant progress towards a relevant professional qualification that meets Central Bank Minimum Competency Requirements. * Previous administration experience an advantage * Excellent attention to detail * Has excellent inter\-personal skills * Ability to work in a dynamic team environment * Be well organized, results driven and capable of working to tight deadlines * Good communicator with excellent interpersonal skills **Additional Information** Primary work location is *Blackrock, Co.Dublin* . The work is office based for at least the first four to six months. If you value an exciting and varied working environment and meet the above requirements, then our recruiter is looking forward to receiving your application by clicking on the button “Apply online”. **Who We Are** Zurich is one of Ireland's leading insurance companies providing a wide range of general insurance and life insurance products and services. The company employs over 1,000 people across its locations in Dublin and Wexford. Zurich is a strong brand – with our 53,000 employees worldwide form the basis of our success, helping our customers in 210 countries and territories to understand and protect themselves from risk. In order to deliver our services, we offer our employees flexible working models and interesting opportunities for further training \& development. As a Zurich employee you benefit from a multitude of advantages as well as a strong culture, characterized by acceptance, diversity and team spirit. At Zurich, we foster a culture of diversity and inclusion. Our purpose and values are designed to protect, inspire confidence and help our employees reach their full potential. We value and defend what is right and promote opportunities for equity among our professionals, regardless of gender, disability, LGBTQ \+, race, ethnicity, generations, belief, etc. Join Zurich and be part of this culture. Together we are committed to delivering on our purpose \- Let’s Create a Brighter Future Together!

title: Optical NDE II, Optical Long Haul Network Deployment
description: **Description** Do you like to use networking concepts and system development engineering to deliver simple, sustainable and repeatable solutions? Would you like your work to play a key role in driving the stability and sustainability of next\-generation IP networks? Amazon Web Services is looking for an Optical Network Development Engineer to support and deploy one of the world’s largest and most complex telecommunication networks. Our goal is to become “The Infrastructure Platform” to the world and customers demand the highest quality and reliability for their services. As we expand at a tremendous rate it is our responsibility to maintain that quality and reliability. We look for innovative ways to automate and scale our network as we expand, while driving complex issues to resolution. AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help. You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion. Key job responsibilities As an Optical Deployment Engineer working within AWS Infrastructure you will: * Design of optical solutions, hands\-on to support deployment and manage rollout of AWS global DWDM network; * Drive optical scaling tasks including fiber quality reviews and generate cutsheets and configs to meet the demand of AWS customers; * Define and configure management solution to support optical network remote monitoring and device visibility; * Work closely with other AWS Networking teams (field tech and fiber teams) to ensure optical network is scaled in time to support business; * Review and implement changes on optical systems using manual CLI config or automated process via script; * Ensure that optical network deployment tasks adhere to the defined AWS network standards; * Execute implementation tasks following operational excellence standards and propose improvements in AWS DWDM network quality and reliability; * Design and implement scalable operational processes to sustain and expand our optical network infrastructure; * Lead end\-to\-end product evaluation and drive strategic commercial tender processes, ensuring optimal vendor selection and cost efficiency * Troubleshoot complex problems on optical networks and eventually propose innovative solutions to improve the deployment agility; * Lead and execute operational excellence programs that measurably improve DWDM network quality and reliability, reducing service\-impacting events and raising the bar on optical network performance; Our ideal candidate is highly autonomous, possesses strong written and verbal communication skills, strong project management and time management skills and has a successful track record of troubleshooting complex network problems and delivering innovative solutions. The desire and ability to work in a fast paced, collaborative environment is essential. This is an amazing opportunity in terms of responsibility, interesting challenges and high visibility. A day in the life This role oversees deployment of Metro and Long Haul systems and all processes related to those deliverables. It includes the design of the projects, generation of all technical documents, updating internal tools, deployment of systems in the real network and handover for operations. Also the deployment team is responsible to participate in testing new solutions and define technical requirements. Automation is a very important topic in this team to improve the deployment process, reduce the manual activities and make interaction between internal tools. **About The Team** Internet Edge provides high\-performing Internet connectivity for a wide range of use cases, such as retail (amazon.com), video streaming (Prime Video), content delivery (CloudFront), DNS (Route53\), AWS services (such as EC2/S3\), consumer devices (such as FireTV/Kindle/Echo), and more. Within AWS Networking, the Internet Edge organization is responsible for connecting Amazon to the global Internet. With connections to thousands of unique external networks in hundreds of locations across the world, our mission is to deliver an Internet connectivity experience that sets the global standard for performance, availability, security, and cost, making Amazon the clear choice for our customers’ needs. The Long Haul Deployment team is responsible for the installation and commissioning of the DWDM systems for Optical Backbone. Our mission is to deliver unconstrained inter\-regional transport at the lowest cost, with the most available product conceivable to AWS customers, making it invisible. The team vends capacity to internet backbone customers that provides low latency, high availability and supports features our customers expect. **About AWS** Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying. Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses. Inclusive Team Culture AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee\-led and company\-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do. Mentorship \& Career Growth We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge\-sharing, mentorship and other career\-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better\-rounded professional. Work/Life Balance We value work\-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve. **Basic Qualifications** * Bachelor's degree or equivalent in a technology field * 4\+ years' experience with major DWDM platforms; * 4\+ years' experience with optical planning, modelling tools and hands\-on implementation; * Experience with the rollout of large DWDM networks in Metro and Long Haul spaces; **Preferred Qualifications** * Knowledge of networking fundamentals * Knowledge of network design, protocols and Layer 1/2 troubleshooting * Experience with optical planning and modelling tools; and the rollout of large DWDM networks. * Optical network design, planning and implementation skills; Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates. Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how\-we\-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. **Company** \- Amazon Development Centre Ireland Limited Job ID: A10425491

title: Order Management Specialist
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Operations Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all. Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce. **Overview** The **Order Management Specialist (OMS)** will be responsible for reviewing and booking all quotes accepted within Salesforce.com compliance policies. In addition to quote conversion, the OMS will manage a personal case queue and may collaborate on special projects as required. The OMS must be able to work in a team environment, be self\-motivated, extremely detail\-oriented with demonstrated problem solving and decision\-making skills. **Key Responsibilities** * Review and process quotes to ensure compliance with Salesforce.com policies. * Adjust opportunities as per given guidelines to ensure accuracy for forecasting purposes * Respond to process \& policy questions and effectively communicate requirements * Manage queues and share best practices and knowledge as one Global Order Management Team * Establish strong relationships with Sales Operations, Renewals, Collections, Compensation, Legal, Sales, Provisioning, * Services and other internal resources as appropriate * Participate in new process development and process improvement projects as well as other ad hoc projects **Required Skills / Experience** * 2\+ years’ Order Management, Customer Service or Sales Support experience. Prior Salesforce.com application experience a plus * Must have strong research and problem\-solving abilities * Excellent interpersonal skills; ability to articulate verbally and written (in English), ability to think quickly * Ability to excel in a fast paced environment, delivering accuracy while managing deadlines and KPIs * Must be able to maintain a positive attitude in stressful situations * Must be available for flexible hours during peak business periods (ME/QE) If you have the necessary skills and passion for order management, we would love to have you join our Ohana! Unleash Your Potential When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form. Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options. Posting Statement Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. title: Order Management Specialist

portfolio **Maintenance of standards and policies** * Align our clients third\-party management standards with Group and CBI regulatory requirements, including OSP and ICT provider governance arrangements, and provide implementation guidance to stakeholders * Support the implementation and embedding of the third\-party management lifecycle framework in collaboration with Compliance, Risk, Microsoft Office and proficiency in procurement management tools * Fluent in English; fluency in French is an advantage *Interested?, please submit your CV through LinkedIn or to* title: Physical Security Engineer
description: **Overview** **Business Function Overview:** In alignment with our Microsoft values, we are committed to cultivating an inclusive work environment for all employees to positively impact our culture every day and we need you as a **Physical Security Engineer** . Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a **Physical Security Engineer** , you will perform a key role in delivering \& sustaining physical security infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including CoPilot, Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. This will include managing the health of physical security systems across a portfolio of locations through break/fix operations, participating in on\-call DRI rotation to provide support, ensuring that systems \& hardware are configured in alignment with baseline standards, participate in program improvements in partnership with other teams, and enabling the delivery of new datacenter capacity through programming access control and video systems. This is a high visibility position in an area of large and expanding investment for Microsoft and offers a terrific opportunity for technical and career growth. This is a flexible work opportunity role offering remote work from home or in person work as desired. We are focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offer trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Microsoft’s Cloud Operations \& Innovation (CO\+I) is the engine that powers our cloud services. As a CE Platform Engineer you will perform a key role in delivering the core infrastructure and foundational technologies for Microsoft's online services including Bing, Office 365, Xbox, OneDrive, and the Microsoft Azure platform. As a group, CO\+I is focused on the personal and professional development for all employees and offers trainings and growth opportunities including Career Rotation Programs, Diversity \& Inclusion trainings and events, and professional certifications. Our infrastructure is comprised of a large global portfolio of more than 100 datacenters and 1 million servers. Our foundation is built upon and managed by a team of subject matter experts working to support services for more than 1 billion customers and 20 million businesses in over 90 countries worldwide. With environmental sustainability and optimization at the forefront of our datacenter design and operations, we continue to grow and evolve as we meet the ever\-changing business demands that hold Microsoft as a world\-class cloud provider. Do you want to empower billions across the world? Come and join us in CO\+I and be at the forefront of the action! **Responsibilities** **Physical Security System Implementation** * Program physical access control and video systems as part of the delivery of new datacenters. * Provide oversight of security systems design for access control and video programming to ensure compliance with design standards and requirements. **Maintaining and Operating Physical Security Systems** * Participate in on\-call DRI rotation to support physical security systems. * Assess data related to the health of security systems and infrastructure to identify gaps, performance trends, compliance with system configuration standards, and work with peers to recommend and execute repeatable strategies to address issues at scale. * Continuously identify and recommend improvements across the program to processes, procedures, and documentation. * Manage physical security systems lifecycle support and compliance, hosting regular datacenter site meetings in partnership with security operations and facility management teams to proactively address site security system health, and coordinate maintenance and repair services with internal and external service providers. **Collaboration And Partner Experience** * Collaborate with partner engineering, project management, and design teams on initiatives that help to improve the physical security environment and improve the overall effectiveness of the program. **Other** * Embody our culture and values. **Qualifications** **Required Qualifications:** * Relevant Experience in physical security, large\-scale computing, modeling, cyber security, anomaly detection, Security Operations Center (SOC) detection, threat analytics, security incident, and event management (SIEM), information technology (IT), and operations incident response. * Bachelor's Degree in Electronic or Electrical Engineering, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science or related field OR equivalent experience. **Background Check Requirements** Ability to meet Microsoft, customer and/or government security screening requirements are required for this role. These requirements include, but are not limited to the following specialized security screenings: * Microsoft Cloud Background Check: This position will be required to pass the Microsoft Cloud background check upon hire/transfer and every two years thereafter. **Preferred Qualifications** * Lenel Certifications, Milestones Certifications, CISSP, CISA, CISM, SANS, GCIA, GCIH, OSCP, and/or Security\+ certification. This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled. Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about **requesting accommodations.**

We’re a fast\-growing, PE\-backed business with a clear focus on doing things well — building great products, acquiring complementary businesses, and continuously investing in our people, processes, and technology. It’s an exciting place to be, and we’re looking for people who want to grow with us. **The Opportunity** Bright is building a dedicated Pre\-Sales function. We are building this team from the ground up and this role will help define what great pre\-sales looks like at Bright, not just fill a seat in an existing structure. Our customers are accountants and payroll professionals. They’re experts in their field and they know immediately whether the person demonstrating software understands their world. This role exists to make sure every prospect conversation is led by someone who does. **The Role** The Pre Sales Specialist sits within our Sales organisation, dedicated to bringing Bright’s product suite to life for customers. You will be involved across the full sales cycle – from discovery through to proposal and close — and your domain knowledge will be central to how we win new business. You will bring solid experience in an accountancy or payroll SaaS environment, and a genuine understanding of the challenges practices and bureaus face day\-to\-day. If you've worked in practice or a bureau yourself, even better — but what matters most is that you can speak to our customers with credibility and meet them as a peer rather than a vendor. This is a consultative, client\-facing role for someone who combines that industry knowledge with genuine product expertise and thrives on helping customers find the right solution. **What Success Looks Like** * Prospects consistently leave demos feeling understood, their specific workflows and challenges reflected in how Bright’s products are presented * Win rates improve as pre\-sales becomes a credible, consistent part of the sales cycle * The Sales team have a go\-to resource they want in the room for complex or high\-value opportunities * Pre\-sales materials, demo environments, and playbooks are continuously improved and embedded across the team **What You’ll Do** * Partner with the Sales team to qualify opportunities and lead technical consultative discovery sessions with prospective clients, understanding their workflows, challenges and requirements * Lead compelling, tailored product knowledge sessions with prospective and existing customers, helping them understand how Bright’s suite addresses the specific challenges of their practice or bureau * Own your product knowledge, maintaining deep, current expertise across the full Bright suite, including integrations, workflows, and competitive positioning. * Translate customer workflow and experience insight into clear, evidenced feedback for Product — articulating not just what customers ask for, but why, and what impact it has on their practice or bureau * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of competitor products and market trends to position Bright’s solutions effectively * Support a smooth handover to Implementation and Customer Success post\-sale, ensuring a seamless experience for new clients * Contribute to the continuous improvement of pre\-sales materials and internal enablement — helping to build the function **What We’re Looking For** **Essential** * Solid experience in an accountancy or payroll SaaS environment, you understand the day\-to\-day realities of practices and bureaus and can speak to customers with genuine credibility. * A consultative, solution\-oriented approach with strong listening and discovery skills and the ability to manage multiple opportunities simultaneously in a fast\-paced environment * Confident navigating multiple software products \- you pick up new tools quickly and evaluate them critically * Demonstrable ability to translate complex product and workflow knowledge into compelling, accessible narratives for both technical and non\-technical audiences * A proven track record of building credibility and trust with customers, prospects, and internal stakeholders * A natural curiosity about how software works and how it could work better, you notice friction, ask why, and have strong instincts about what a good product experience looks like **Nice to Have** * Familiarity with Bright’s product suite (BrightPay, BrightBooks, BrightAP, or similar) * A background in an accountancy practice or a payroll bureau, you’ve sat on the other side of the software * Exposure to AI\-assisted workflows or tools **Working at Bright** Bright is built around brilliant people doing brilliant work, and we invest in making sure the environment matches that ambition. You’ll have genuine flexibility in how you work, backed by modern practices that adapt to how work actually gets done today. We recognise and celebrate strong performance, support your wellbeing, and give you real opportunities to grow, not just in role, but in career. We’re proud of the culture we’ve built. It’s high\-trust, high\-expectation, and focused on doing great work. ***That’s Bright***

title: Primary Education Officer
description: Post\-Primary Languages Ireland are currently hiring for a Primary Education Officer to join their team. **About The Role:** =================== PPLI/Languages Connect is seeking to recruit an Education Officer with competence in a foreign language to support the implementation of Languages Connect – Ireland’s Strategy for Foreign Languages in Education, diversifying, enhancing and expanding the teaching of foreign languages. The candidate for this role will be required to be qualified as a primary school teacher, have excellent leadership and interpersonal skills, be able to motivate and inspire teachers and work with teams of associates. Flexibility and an ability to engage in an evolving programme of work in a culture of improvement, collaboration, innovation, and creativity are required. **Key Responsibilities:** ------------------------- * Prepare detailed breakdown of deliverables and deadlines for projects * Oversee and manage the achievement of deliverables of projects * Manage, develop and enhance the Foreign Language Upskilling Scheme, by engaging with a range of providers, including Education Support Centres, HEIs, Cultural Institutes and Embassies * Evaluate the impact of programmes * Provide advice and training to tutors recruited to deliver upskilling courses, where relevant * Maintain effective communication with all course providers and teachers/schools * Deliver information sessions to hosting class teachers and school management teams in the context of Say Yes to Languages * Develop, prepare, and deliver resources and supports for teaching and learning to support delivery of MFL curriculum * Assess teachers’ needs via research and surveys * Establish and maintain working relationships with schools, Department of Education, and all other relevant stakeholders * Manage training of, and collaboration with, associates * Support the delivery of messages to the wider public through articles, presentations and newsletter items. Prepare project reports and conduct evaluation of programmes * Represent PPLI/Languages Connect at events such as conferences, give presentations, facilitate workshops **Desirable Skills and Qualifications:** ---------------------------------------- * Hold a qualification in a foreign language and/or a relevant qualification; * Irish language skills to carry out work through the medium of Irish; * Have familiarity with CEFR and CEFR Companion Volume. **Required Competencies:** -------------------------- * Project management (including planning \& organisation, ability to multi\-task, prioritise where necessary, flexibility, working independently and as part of a team) * Judgement, analysis and decision making * Management and delivery of Results * Leadership, including interpersonal skills, motivating/inspiring others both within the team and with external stakeholders * Research and Report writing * Interpersonal and communication skills, including competence to engage stakeholders and partners * Professional knowledge, expertise, and self\-development, including digital expertise and capacity to prepare and deliver workshops, training and presentations ***Deadline for applications is 12pm on Tuesday, 2nd June 2026\.*** For more information on the role, please see the Information Booklet. To apply, please fill out the Application Form. **About PPLI:** =============== Post\-Primary Languages Ireland (PPLI) provides expertise and support for foreign language education in Ireland, fully funded by the Department of Education. Along with a growing number of partners, PPLI also delivers the Languages Connect awareness campaign, which highlights the benefits of foreign language skills.

Please complete your AI interview and application steps to be considered for this opportunity.*

title: Problem Analyst
description: **Job Title:** Problem Analyst **Reports To:** Manager, Problem Management Team **Department:** Technology Operations **Location:** Dundalk, Co. Louth **Hours of Work:** 37\.5 hours weekly **Summary** We are seeking a highly motivated and self\-managed individual to become part of a global team, responsible for the management and resolution of system problems. This includes but is not limited to taking ownership of problem cases which have been escalated by other support teams, managing and resolving system outages, and identifying and driving application defects to resolution. The Problem Analyst should be acutely aware of the company’s operational needs and be able to prioritize all work based on business impact. You should possess excellent oral and written communication skills, an ability to present and discuss technical information with all levels of the business in a way that establishes rapport, persuades others, and gains understanding. You should have a strong background in Windows products and applications, as well as Cloud Computing platforms such as Microsoft Azure, with proficiency in troubleshooting and diagnosing technical issues, specifically troubleshooting servers, databases, applications, network issues, and the overall interaction between each of these layers. Experience in MSSQL is also essential. This position provides the opportunity to work on global business systems and the ability to learn and develop new skills while working on projects. **Duties \& Responsibilities** * Be self\-managed to react to and provide support to both technical and non\-technical teams. * Learn and become subject matter expert on in\-house applications as well as standard enterprise systems. * Take ownership of problems, working with engineering and development teams in order to provide problem resolutions and appropriate proactive monitoring. * Be a point of escalation for second level support teams. * Provide expertise on complex issues and technology projects. * Prioritize, triage and take ownership of escalated problems (PRs). * Troubleshoot escalated issues and system outages, driving each incident to a resolution. * Provide timely, accurate business communication to stakeholders regarding critical problems/outages and provide root cause and business impact details. * Provide Root Cause Analysis reports to stakeholders and management teams. * Identity and log application defects. Manage defects and drive to resolution wherever possible. * Analyse current applications/systems and processes to facilitate automation and process improvement. * Create and update Knowledge Base (KB) articles for workarounds and known errors which are identified throughout the lifecycle of a system problem. * Drive continuous improvements on current processes, methodology and applications to increase product quality and efficiency **Essential Criteria** * Excellent problem solving and troubleshooting with a high aptitude for complex systems. * Bachelor’s Degree in related field or relevant industry experience. * Understand current processes and offer improvements. * Support our global infrastructure and multiple in\-house applications. * Ability to prioritize and handle multiple issues * Excellent communication both oral and written * Sound decision making with the ability to work under minimum supervision * Detail oriented and attention to detail * Troubleshooting 3 Tier architecture (web servers, app servers, databases) * Microsoft Server (Windows Server) * Database Systems (Microsoft SQL) * SQL or similar language * Should have some experience working with XML, JSON and RESTful APIs. * Automation/Scripting – PowerShell or similar language * Enterprise Monitoring Software Experience (OBM, Sitescope, New Relic, Datadog) * Adhering to high financial penalty SLAs * Microsoft Azure (Azure Portal, Service Bus, Cosmos DB) * Some exposure to Scrum methodologies. **Desired Criteria** * Microsoft Development Suite (.NET, Java, ASP) * Microsoft IIS * VMWare * Network Technologies (routing, switching, F5 load balancing, Cisco) * Wireshark or other Traffic Capture tools * Security (Firewall, VPN) * Production environment experience * Salesforce * Microsoft Azure DevOps * Artificial intelligence **Employee Benefits** * Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Life Assurance * Employee Health and Wellbeing Initiatives * Enhanced Annual Leave * 24/7 Employee Assistance Programme * Enhanced Maternity/Paternity Leave * Social Club * Free carparking

title: Production Operator
description: Manufacturing Biotech Associate (BTA) Level 02 – Job Description Job Summary The Manufacturing Biotech Associate (BTA) Level 02 is responsible for supporting biologic drug substance manufacturing operations within a state\-of\-the\-art GMP facility. The role involves operating automated manufacturing systems, performing upstream and downstream processing activities, setting up single\-use equipment, and ensuring all production activities are carried out safely, efficiently, and in compliance with regulatory standards. The position supports both clinical and commercial biologics manufacturing in a multi\-product environment and requires flexibility, teamwork, and strong attention to quality and safety. Key Responsibilities * Support end\-to\-end biologics manufacturing operations. * Operate manufacturing equipment according to electronic batch records (EBRs), sampling plans, and standard operating procedures (SOPs). * Perform upstream and downstream processing activities including inoculation, cell culture, filtration, and purification. * Set up and prepare single\-use systems and manufacturing equipment between production runs. * Ensure strict adherence to GMP, EHS, contamination control, and compliance standards. * Complete manufacturing documentation accurately and on time. * Participate in shift handovers, production meetings, and team discussions. * Escalate operational, safety, or compliance issues to supervisors when necessary. * Support continuous improvement projects using Lean Six Sigma methodologies. * Help achieve key performance indicators (KPIs) related to safety, production, compliance, training, and equipment effectiveness. * Create and update SOPs and work instructions to maintain regulatory compliance. * Maintain technical knowledge through continuous training and development. * Work collaboratively within cross\-functional manufacturing teams. Required Skills \& Qualifications Education * Level 6 qualification in Science, Engineering, Biotechnology, Biochemistry, Pharmaceutical Science, or a related discipline preferred. Experience * Minimum 2 years of experience in a GMP manufacturing environment or equivalent experience. - Technical Skills * Understanding of biologics or monoclonal antibody (mAb) manufacturing processes. * Familiarity with upstream and downstream unit operations. * Experience with single\-use technologies in manufacturing environments. * Knowledge of contamination control and batch release requirements. * Familiarity with automation systems such as: * Emerson DeltaV * PAS\-X * Electronic batch record systems Soft Skills * Strong teamwork and communication skills. * Ability to work independently and within cross\-functional teams. * Strong attention to detail and compliance awareness. * Problem\-solving and continuous improvement mindset. * Flexibility to work in a fast\-paced manufacturing environment. Working Conditions * Site\-based manufacturing role. * Requires 24/7 shift work, including nights and weekends. * Work performed in GMP cleanroom and manufacturing environments.

title: Project Ecologist
description: **About us** MKO is Ireland’s largest Ecology and Environmental consultancy. Our team of 200\+ experienced professionals work across the ecology, planning, environmental, and related fields. We deliver challenging and complex projects for our clients and have an unrivalled track record of success. We add real value to our client’s projects through our multi\-disciplinary expertise and a broad range of nationwide experience. Our success and growth rests entirely on the knowledge, skills, ambition and integrity of our people, and we value these qualities most highly. We are forward\-thinking and work with a can\-do attitude, which nurtures people, ideas and opportunities to help maximise potential. Through our work, we aim to leave a legacy we can all be proud of. **Job Description** We are recruiting a Project Ecologist to join our expanding Ecology team of over 90 Ecologists delivering projects nationwide. This role offers the opportunity to contribute to a diverse portfolio of projects across Ireland, spanning the renewable energy, transport, water and infrastructure sectors, as well as a range of private sector developments. You will apply the skills developed through formal education, field experience and previous roles, while continuing to grow professionally within a supportive, multidisciplinary environment. **What you’ll bring to the role:** As a Project Ecologist, you will play a key role in the planning and delivery of ecology projects, including: * Scoping, planning and undertaking ecological surveys in the terrestrial, freshwater or marine environments. * Managing ecology projects from inception to completion * Liaising with clients, project stakeholders and multidisciplinary project teams * Preparing written ecological reports, impact assessments, management plans, etc, as the output of field survey efforts, including mapping, data collation, etc. * Preparing the scope of work and fee proposal documents for new projects. * Prepare high‑quality report outputs within set deadlines. * Mentoring and supporting junior members of the Ecology team * Managing project budgets to ensure the scope of work is completed in the allocated time. * Prioritising assigned workload while balancing client expectations, available resources and project deadlines. **What we’re looking for in you:** * A third\-level qualification in ecology, botany, natural sciences, or other related fields that give the candidate the necessary skills to fulfil the role. * A minimum of **4\+ years of post\-graduate experience** in fulfilling similar functions, ideally in a similar consultancy role. * A broad range of ecological survey and reporting experience. * Excellent report writing skills and proven project management abilities. * An excellent understanding of the EU Habitats and EIA Directives in so far as they relate to project\-based ecological impact assessments being undertaken by an ecological consultancy. * Demonstrated people management and/or mentoring capability. * Full driving licence. **Desirable** * Associate or Full Member of CIEEM (other a relevant professional body) or working towards the same. We offer a competitive salary and benefits package, recognising the contribution you will make. At MKO, your professional development is important to us, with ongoing opportunities for growth, training and leadership development. MKO offers a culture that supports you. We actively embrace diversity and inclusion as fundamental elements of our workplace culture. We welcome candidates from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives, recognising that a diverse team fosters innovation, creativity, and a more dynamic work environment. We promote a workplace where everyone feels valued, respected, and empowered to contribute their unique strengths. Candidates interested in the available position should apply by emailing an up\-to\-date Curriculum Vitae and cover letter. **About us** https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=50vRU\_YIbo0

title: Project Engineer
description: Elusav Recruitment have a great opportunity for an experienced Project Engineer to join my clients teams on one of Ireland largest Waste Water treatment facilities, where the organisation are are responsible for the overall up time, sustaining and Capex projects for the site. As a Project Engineer who will work with Maintenance Manager on mostly capital maintenance projects and SLA/Contractor management related to maintenance activities. This role will involve delivering multiple small,multidisciplinary projects with a combined annual value in excess of €3m. There is also a clear progression path, with the intention for the successful candidate to move into a Project Manager role under the Maintenance Manager in the future. Permanent position with an expanding site that is using advanced tech to sustainably generate it's own energy; while treating water that is crucial to the ecosystem and population of Dublin. A company that has been crucial to the design, build, operation and maintenance of Ireland's most critical WWTP for the past 20\+ years'. **Responsibilities:** * production of documentation, including reports, drawings, calculations, specifications, tender documentation including particular specifications. * production of project budgets and resource loaded detailed work programmes. * co\-ordinating with PSCS, PSDP, Client, Operations and other stakeholders. * providing technical advice and guidance. * providing input to bids and project manage appropriate projects. * supervision \& management of contractors. * responding to Client requests for information. * Preparation and/or review RAMS. * Identify interfaces with operations, impacts, consequence, work\-around's and plan, execute operations dependent tasks within the suitable window. * Support maintenance by managing SLAs and contractors. * Identify and prioritise capital maintenance projects based on equipment/process condition and criticality. - **Requirements:** * The ability to command respect and to create a sense of community amongst the members of the project teams. * Ability risk assess equipment and criticality. * have experience of design at all levels from feasibility to a "for construction" level. * PRINCE2 or PMP qualification or experience in a PRINCE2/PMP structured projects is preferred. - **Contact:** If you would like to learn more about the vacancy, apply now, or contact Brendan Mullins at bmullins@elusav.com or \+353 61 527 150\.

title: Project Engineer - MEP
description: **Overview** PM Group's Outsourced Technical Services (OTS) department are seeking to recruit a MEP Project Engineer to be based on our client facility in South Dublin. The MEP Project Engineer will be responsible for leading and coordinating the entire project lifecycle from initiation through planning, execution, commissioning, and close‑out. While not acting as the primary technical designer, this role ensures the successful integration of engineering, manufacturing, quality, and site activities, driving projects to completion safely, on time, and within budget. The Senior Project Engineer serves as the operational backbone of project delivery, ensuring smooth communication, risk management, commercial control, and high‑quality outcomes reporting project status to the Project Manager. **Responsibilities** * Lead day‑to‑day project coordination across all lifecycle phases. * Oversee planning, scheduling, sequencing, and interfacing between internal teams and the client. * Manage project documentation, reporting, risk logs, and progress metrics. * Drive commercial awareness—tracking costs, managing variations, and supporting budget adherence. * Act as the primary operational point of contact for client, general contractor, and subcontractors. * Ensure all project deliverables meet quality, safety, and compliance requirements. * Identify and address issues early, escalating to the Project Manager as required. * Continuously refine project delivery processes and execution methodology. * Support commissioning readiness and close‑out documentation, ensuring all lifecycle gates are completed correctly. * Providing project‑driven feedback on technical drawings and installation methodology. * Supporting commissioning planning and readiness reviews. * Guide junior project engineers in project administration and workflow management. **Qualifications** Qualifications / Training Required\* * Degree in Engineering (Mechanical, Electrical, Building Services, or related). * Strong technical problem‑solving ability. * Demonstrated capability delivering high‑quality engineered solutions in demanding environments. * 5–7 years in a project engineering or site management role within manufacturing, construction, or a technical project delivery, experience managing activities on complex projects. * Additional project management or leadership training a plus **Skills / Experience** * Strong competency in project planning, technical coordination, and cross‑functional leadership. * Proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Project. * Excellent communication skills—verbal, written, and technical documentation. * Proven ability to mentor and support junior team members. Desirable * Experience with HVAC delivery or projects within data centre or industrial environments. * Proficiency in reading and interpreting engineering drawings, P\&IDs, and electrical schematics. * Experience implementing continuous improvement or lean methodologies. * Ability to build team morale and lead through influence.

title: Quantity Surveyor
